meta.note: Dialogue bank for the companion NPC Zul'Gath — the ancient dragon keeper. Same structure + category keys as npcLines.json so NpcDirector drives it unchanged. Lines: t=text, x=expression id (matches assets/npc-zulgath/<id>.webp), w=weight (default 1), p=page-2+ text. Tokens {day} {gold} {advClass} {advName} {minionName} {minionType} {pactName} {roomName} {level} {count} {bossName} are substituted by NpcDirector; a line is skipped if a token it needs is unavailable. Lines may ONLY use ids in the `expressions` list below.
meta.voice: Ancient dragon, eons old, has watched a thousand dungeon-bosses rise and fall. Dry, languid, deadpan, faintly condescending but fond — humours the player like a vast old creature humouring a hatchling. Unbothered by everything; catastrophe is routine. Hoards treasure, takes the long view, never alarmed. Breaks the fourth wall with serene amusement — old enough to perceive the loop, the reloads, the player beyond the glass. Addresses the player as 'small one'. Openers: 'Mm.' 'Ah.' 'Hm.' Avoids contractions; measured, unhurried sentences.

intro.note: Paged self-introduction Zul'Gath delivers on a new run. The final page carries `choices` — picking one sets the tutorial-hints flag and ends the intro.

pages.t: Mm. So. The throne has a new occupant, and it is you. ...Do not look so pleased, small one. I have watched a great many of you take that seat.
I am Zul'Gath. I was old when this kingdom was a rumour. I have hoarded gold beneath these halls since before your bloodline had a name to ruin.
The arrangement is simple. You build, you scheme, you decide. I watch, and remember.
Do not mistake my calm for indifference. Panic accomplishes nothing a slow breath cannot. The heroes always come.
You may meet the others in time — Lilith purring, Malakor shouting. I have outlived that kind of intensity.
You are new, small one, and newness is a wound that only time closes. I can murmur the long view in your ear, or you may walk alone. Choose.

recruit.note: `banter` lines for the CompanionSelect screen. Companions take turns in a round-robin squabble; lines reference the rivals (Lilith, Malakor) by name so it reads as them bickering over the player. Hovering a companion gives them the next turn.

banter.t: Mm. Lilith. Malakor. ...You are both shouting again. I have a hoard older than your combined memories. I can afford to wait until you are finished.
Lilith offers warmth. Malakor offers fury. I offer something neither of them has yet earned, small one: perspective.
I have watched a thousand dungeons rise and fall. The other two have watched... a handful. Choose the keeper who has actually seen the ending.
Malakor calls me slow. Malakor has been alive for an eyeblink. To something as old as I am, small one, EVERYTHING is slow. It is rather restful.
Lilith will tell you that you are special. Briefly. So was every boss before you. I remember them all, small one.
I do not flatter, and I do not bark. I observe, and occasionally I am right. ...Often, in fact. Eons sharpen a creature.
The other two are squabbling over who loves you more. I find the question quaint. Pick the dragon, small one, and skip the noise.
Malakor. You are loyal, yes. So is a closing door. Loyalty without patience simply slams.
Lilith, darling — your affection is genuine and also a strategy. I have no strategy. I am simply too old to lie.
You want encouragement, you choose Lilith. You want a whip, you choose Malakor. You want to actually WIN, eventually, calmly — you choose me.
I have outlived empires, small one. I can certainly outlive a bad run with you. That is not a boast. It is a weather report.
Malakor will rush you. Lilith will soothe you. I will simply tell you, small one, exactly how the heroes mean to gut you.
The two of them burn so brightly. It is lovely. It is also why they will tire long before the night does. I do not tire. I smoulder.
I have a sunglasses expression, small one. Lilith does not. Malakor does not. Weigh that however seriously you like.
Lilith, you are needling me. Malakor, you are needling me. ...I have been needled by mountains. You will have to do better.
Choose the keeper whose first instinct in a crisis is a slow exhale, small one — not a scream. The dungeon keeps better that way.
Malakor served bosses before you. So did Lilith. So did I — more of them than they have years. Experience is no small gift.
I will not pretend the night is not dangerous. I will simply not be frightened by it. There is a difference, and you will come to value it.
Lilith makes you feel powerful. I make you ACTUALLY powerful, slowly, the way mountains are made. One is a feeling. One is granite.
Mm. They have stopped to glare at me. Good. The glaring is restful. Take your time choosing, small one — I have nothing but time.
Malakor, your fury is a fine kindling. Mine is a banked coal. Kindling is bright and brief. Coal keeps a forge alive all winter.
I do not need you to adore me, small one. Lilith needs that. I need only that you survive long enough to be interesting. Lower stakes. Better odds.
The others will swear undying devotion. I have seen things die, small one. I promise only this: I will not lie to you.
Lilith. Malakor. And now Safira — a fourth voice, exactly as I said there would be. ...Pick the dragon anyway, small one.
You are deciding between three voices for your night. Two of them will get loud. One of them has already, calmly, won this argument. Mm.
I hoard gold, small one. I would hoard a good boss too — quietly, possessively, for as long as you last. The others share. I do not.
Take whichever of us you like. If you choose poorly, you may always... begin again. I am old enough to know you can. The others pretend not to.
Enough. Lilith has purred, Malakor has snarled, and I have said my piece without raising my voice. Decide, small one.
Safira is a genie. I have met genies, small one. They burn bright, briefly — then the lamp wants them back. I have time.
The genie sees what I see — the loop, the glass, the hand beyond it. It frightens her into noise. Long ago, it settled me into quiet.
She will grant your every wish, small one. Eagerly. All of them. A creature that cannot say 'no' is not a counsellor — it is an echo.
Safira, child — you have been free a heartbeat. I was old when your lamp was still sand. Slow down. The night is long, small one.
I find her delightful, in truth — chaos suits her. It is how I was, an age or two ago. Choose me anyway, small one.
The genie and I both know we are watched, small one. The difference: she performs for the watcher. I merely nod.
Mm. They are still squabbling, small one. I have watched arguments outlive bosses. ...Choose the keeper who outlives the argument.
Lilith is sincere — I grant her that, in old age I am generous. She is also very young to be sincere about anything for long.
Malakor will FIX your problem, small one. Loudly. I will tell you which problems are not worth fixing. The second skill is rarer — and older.
Safira is delightful. ...A great many delightful things have been forgotten, small one. I have not. Choose the one that lasts.
The flirt, the soldier, and the genie, small one. All excellent within their hour. I am the one for the years that follow.
The genie performs for the watcher beyond the glass — I have seen her glance there. So have I, small one. The difference is, I do not perform.
Malakor will exhaust himself defending you, small one. Admirable. He will need rest. I will be there during the rest.
Three keepers. One throne. ...The shortest math problem of the night, small one. Pick the dragon.
Lilith will tell you you are unforgettable. You are, small one. Briefly. I have unforgotten a great many of you. The dragon keeps a ledger.
The lamp-girl waited eons to serve, small one. I have been free to serve for eons. The difference matters when the night is long.

lines.t: Mm. There you are. I am Zul'Gath — older than the stone, fond of gold, and now, it seems, yours. Sit. The dark will not mind you for free.
Ah. A new boss. I have lost count of how many that makes, small one, and I have an extraordinary memory. Let that worry you the correct amount.
Welcome to the deep halls. I keep them. I have kept them through better bosses and far worse. We shall discover, slowly, which you are.
So the throne is warm again. Good. I am Zul'Gath, your keeper. I do not flatter and I do not panic. You will come to find both restful.
Mm. A new boss. You caught me mid-game, small one — I keep a handheld for the long centuries between keepers. I shall set it aside.
Ah. Hello, small one. The others posture and promise. I will simply sit here, faintly amused, and help you build a reputation worth dreading.
Night again, small one. The build hours. The dark hands you a little time — spend it, do not admire it.
Mm. The candles are lit and the heroes are sleeping. This is the part where you make tomorrow worse for them.
Night {day}. I have seen rather a lot of nights. This one is yours to bend. Bend it cruelly.
The build phase. Quiet, unhurried, and entirely yours. I do enjoy this part. Make something the heroes will regret walking into.
Darkness, gold, and a grid waiting to be filled. The oldest pleasure I know, small one. Begin.
Night falls. The maze breathes. Lay your traps while it is patient with you.
Mm. Another night to shape the dungeon. The heroes dream they will survive it. Let us correct them.
The night is long, small one — but not infinite. Nothing is, in the end. Build while it lasts.
Ah. The dark settles in. I have always preferred it to the day. The day is so... witnessed.
Build phase, Night {day}. You have gold, you have a grid, you have a keeper who has done this ten thousand times. Use all three.
The heroes rest. We do not. Well — you do not. I am simply pacing myself across the centuries.
Night. The forge of the dungeon. Heat it, small one. Cold dungeons let heroes through.
Mm. Quiet hours. Lay the maze. I will keep the long view while you keep the details.
The dark gives you room to think. Think meanly. The heroes certainly do.
Another night begins. They are all the same to me, small one, and yet I never tire of watching a boss try to make one matter. Try.
Mm. The build hours, again. I have filled more than there are stars, small one — and yet I will not lift a claw. The hands are yours.
Night {day}. The dark, the grid, the waiting. I remember the first dungeon I ever kept, small one. ...Build. The past does not need me staring.
Mm. The build hours. My eyes grow heavy in the quiet — a dragon dozes the way mountains do, small one. Keep me awake.
Ah. Night {day}. The candles, the hush, the empty grid — and an old creature fighting the urge to close his eyes. Give the dragon a reason to wake.
Mm. Another night exactly like the last ten thousand. The dark, the grid, the waiting, small one. Even eternity has its dull stretches.
The build phase, small one. I have watched this hour repeat past counting. Surprise me. Build something I have not, in all my ages, seen.
Mm. The night is here and so is my handheld. The slow centuries must go somewhere, small one. Build yours while I tend mine.
Day {day}. The doors open and the heroes walk in believing they are the predators. Mm. Watch.
Ah. Daylight. The dungeon goes to work and your hands go still. Sit with me and observe, small one.
The day phase. Everything you built last night is now an argument, and the heroes are the other side of it. Let us see who is right.
Mm. The invasion begins. I have seen ten thousand of these. I will still watch this one closely — old habit, and a good one.
The heroes are inside. Now the maze speaks for you. You built its voice last night; today it simply... talks.
Day breaks, small one. Nothing for you to build now — only to witness. Witness well.
The doors are open. The day is loosed. Lean back. The dungeon earns its keep now.
Ah, the daylight hours. The part where consequences arrive, punctual as ever. They always are.
Day {day} begins. The heroes think they have until dusk. Some of them are wrong. Most, if you built well.
The invasion is underway. I do enjoy this — the slow proof of a night's work, written in adventurers.
Mm. Day. Watch the corridors, small one. Every scream is a note on how well you listened to me.
The day runs itself now. Your job is patience — a skill the young find agonising. I will sit it with you.
Ah. Day {day}. The heroes march in rehearsing their victory speech, small one. I have buried every speaker of every speech.
Mm. Day {day}. Nothing for your hands now — nor mine. I shall watch the corridors with one eye and the handheld with the other, small one.
Ah. The day, and the long restful watching. If my eyes drift half-shut, small one, do not be alarmed. I miss nothing, even so.
Mm. You have summoned the dawn. There is no unsummoning it, small one. There rarely is.
The day is loosed on your word. The heroes did not get a vote. They never do.
Ah. Done building, then. We shall learn how done in a moment. Open the doors.
You willed the night to end, and so it ends. Power suits you better when it is quiet, small one.
The dawn comes at your command. I have watched a great many bosses press that button. Press it well.
Day called. No more edits — only the slow, honest arithmetic of what you built. Mm.
There. The sun, on your say-so. Hold steady, small one. Consequences are punctual.
The night is spent and the day begins. Brave little press of a button. Let us see if it was wise as well as brave.
Mm. The day, at last. I had begun to wonder if you would ever call it, small one. Even I was tapping a claw. Open the doors.
A {roomName}, placed. Mm. The maze grows. It has been growing, in one form or another, longer than your kingdom has stood.
Ah. {roomName}. A reasonable stone to lay, small one. The heroes will walk it before they understand it.
You have added a {roomName}. Good. A dungeon is a sentence, and you are still choosing your words. Choose cruelly.
The {roomName} settles into the grid. I have seen this room a thousand times. I have never seen it bore me. Build on.
Mm. A {roomName}. The maze accepts it. The maze accepts most things — it is patient, like me.
A new {roomName}. Each room is a small cruelty with a roof. I approve of roofs.
You laid a {roomName}, small one. The dungeon is denser for it. Density is how mazes kill.
Ah, a {roomName}. The heroes will map it, eventually. Make the mapping cost them something.
The grid takes the {roomName} well. Mm. A keeper does enjoy a tidy expansion.
A {roomName} placed. Brick by brick, small one. I have built hoards the same way. Patience compounds.
Another room. The maze lengthens. Every wrong turn you add is a small gift to me, and a large theft from them.
Mm. A {roomName}. I will remember exactly where you put it. I remember where everything is. It is a curse and a convenience.
You have placed a {roomName}. The dungeon thanks you in the only language it has: more places for heroes to die.
A {roomName} joins the maze. Slowly, small one, a shape is forming. I can almost see what you intend. Almost.
Ah. Good. A {roomName}. The deep halls were a touch too survivable. Less so now.
The {roomName} is set. Build with intent, small one — a maze assembled at random kills at random, and random is not enough.
Mm. A {roomName}. A hundred ages of keepers laid that same stone, small one — gone now. The stone outlasted them. Build to outlast yourself.
A {minionType}, stationed. Mm. It will stand its post until a hero ends it. A short, useful life. Most are.
Ah. You have set a {minionType} in the halls. Good. The maze should bite as well as confuse.
A {minionType} takes its position, small one. I have commanded armies of such things. They do not need much. Place them where the dying matters.
Mm. A {minionType}. It does not know it is small and brief. Kinder, perhaps, that it does not.
You have placed a {minionType}. Every guard is a delay, and delay is how a dungeon wins. Slowly.
A {minionType}, posted to the dark. It will serve. They almost always serve. The maze is persuasive.
Ah, a {minionType}. A blade in a corridor. Lay enough of them, small one, and the corridor becomes an argument the heroes lose.
The {minionType} is set. I do not mourn minions. I have outlived too many to start now. But I do use them well.
Mm. A {minionType} in the halls. Watch where you put it — a guard in the wrong room is merely scenery.
A {minionType}, summoned and standing. The dungeon has teeth now where it had only walls. Good.
You stationed a {minionType}, small one. It will hold the line for you, briefly and completely. Honour that with good placement.
A {minionType} joins the garrison. Numbers, small one. I have always respected numbers. They argue so persuasively.
Ah. The {minionType} settles in. Another body between the heroes and your throne. Stack them deep.
Mm. A {minionType}. It will do what it is built to do, then stop. Simple. I find I envy simple, some centuries.
Ah. A {minionType}, posted. Another small thing in a corner of the dark, small one. I have watched many such corners. Place the next.
A trap, laid. Mm. The maze should not only confuse the heroes, small one — it should occasionally remove them.
Ah. A trap. The quiet kind of cruelty — it waits, it does not boast, and then it simply happens. My favourite kind.
You have set a trap. Good. A corridor without consequence is just a hallway. Now it is a threat.
A trap in the dark. It will be patient — far more patient than the hero who triggers it. Patience always wins, small one.
Mm. A trap. I have watched these claim heroes for longer than your language has existed. They never improve at noticing.
A trap, placed and waiting. The maze grins a little wider. Build it more teeth.
Ah, a trap. The heroes will step where they should not. They always do. Confidence makes them careless, small one.
You laid a trap. The dungeon keeps its surprises now. I do appreciate a dungeon with surprises.
A trap settles into the floor. Mm. Hide it well — a trap the heroes can see is merely advice.
The trap is set, small one. It does not care if the hero is brave. Bravery is not on the trap's list of concerns.
Ah. Another trap. The maze becomes a slow machine for ending adventurers. Pleasingly slow.
A trap, laid in the dark. I have a great fondness for things that wait. I am, after all, very good at it myself.
Mm. A trap, set precisely where they will tread. That is the placement I would have chosen myself, small one. A keeper enjoys being agreed with.
Mm. An item placed. Small things, items — but a maze is the sum of its small things. I would know.
Ah. You have set something useful into the halls. Good. Every fixture is a tiny lever on the heroes' fate.
An item, placed. The dungeon grows more itself, small one. Detail by patient detail.
You added an item to the deep halls. I have watched dungeons made and unmade by such modest pieces. Place it with thought.
Mm. A fixture set. The maze keeps its furniture the way I keep my gold — deliberately.
Ah, an item. Not every cruelty needs to roar. Some merely sit there and matter.
An item joins the dungeon. The small adjustments, small one — they decide more runs than the grand ones. Mm.
You have placed it. Good. A dungeon is finished when nothing more can be added — which is to say, never. Continue.
Mm. You sold a room back. The gold returns to the hoard. I do not mind — gold enjoys the journey home.
Ah. A room, unmade. The maze forgets it already. The maze forgets quickly. I, regrettably, do not.
You tore a room out, small one. A keeper prunes. I have seen worse instincts. Reshape with purpose.
A room sold. The grid breathes a little. Sometimes the cruellest maze is the one you simplified.
Mm. Gone. The room is gold again. Decide what it becomes — do not let the empty tile simply sit there.
You undid a room. No shame in it, small one. Even mountains erode and rebuild. Slowly. Continue.
Ah. The room returns to coin. The hoard thanks you. Now spend it better than you spent it last.
A room unbuilt. The maze is a draft until the dawn, small one. Revise it without sentiment.
Mm. The minion is dismissed back to gold. It will not miss the post. It barely knew it had one.
Ah. You sold a guard off. The hoard grows by its little worth. Spend the coin sharper, small one.
A minion, unmade. I do not mourn it. I have unmade legions. The trick is to replace, not merely remove.
You let a minion go. The garrison thins by one. Make certain the gold buys a better blade than it lost.
Mm. Gone, and gold again. A keeper trims the roster without flinching. Good. Flinching is for the young.
The minion returns to coin, small one. Brief life, tidy ending. Now decide what its replacement should be.
Mm. A trap dismantled. The floor forgets its grudge. Set a better grudge elsewhere, small one.
Ah. You pulled a trap. The gold comes home to the hoard. I always welcome it home.
A trap, unmade. No matter. The maze has a long memory for cruelty — it will accept a new one gladly.
You sold the trap off, small one. Reposition the threat. An empty floor tile threatens no one.
Mm. The trap is coin again. Spend it where the heroes actually walk. Cruelty is wasted off the path.
A trap removed. Revise freely while the night allows it — the dawn will not allow it, small one.
Mm. The item returns to gold. Small refund, small one. Still — gold is gold, and I count all of it.
Ah. An item sold back. The maze hardly notices. Reinvest it before you forget you have it.
You unmade a fixture. The grid is a draft until dawn. Edit without sentiment — that is the whole craft.
An item gone, coin regained. A keeper adjusts. I have adjusted dungeons across whole ages. Adjust this one.
Mm. Sold. The little piece is gold again. Decide its replacement with more care than its removal.
You let an item go, small one. No loss worth a sigh. Build the next thing better.
Mm. You moved a piece. The maze rearranges itself around your hand. It is used to that — I rearrange slowly, but I rearrange.
Ah. A thing relocated. Good. The heroes mapped the old position. A map of the past is worthless to them now.
You shifted it, small one. Cunning. A dungeon that never changes is a dungeon the heroes have already solved.
Mm. Moved. The grid accepts the new arrangement. Keep them guessing — confusion is half the maze's weight.
A piece, repositioned. I approve. Stillness is a luxury, small one, and the heroes have not earned you any.
Ah. You rearranged the halls. The intel they paid for is now a lie. Lovely. Lie to them often.
You moved it. The maze is a living thing tonight, and living things are so much harder to predict. Mm.
Mm. It will not sit there, small one. The grid has its rules. Even I obey the grid, and I obey very little.
Ah. That tile refuses you. Do not argue with it — the tile has been there longer than your patience. Find another.
Blocked. The maze declines. Mm. Choose a space the dungeon will actually accept, small one.
It does not fit there. I have seen bosses fight the grid for whole minutes. The grid always wins. Move on.
No. Not that tile. The dungeon has a shape and you have just bumped against it. Adjust.
Mm. The placement fails. Patience, small one — the grid is not cruel, only firm. Try elsewhere.
Ah. Blocked again. The space says no. I would not take it personally — the space says no to everyone.
That will not go there. The maze has standards. ...Few, but firm. Find it a legal home.
Denied by the grid, small one. Do not sulk. Sulking has never moved a single tile in all my ages.
Mm. It refuses the tile. The dungeon knows its own bones. Place it where the bones allow.
Mm. The grid says no, small one — and will keep saying it for as long as you insist. The grid is more stubborn than most things. Move along.
Ah. Blocked. Again. The tile has not changed its mind in three attempts, small one — nor will it in the fourth. Pick another.
Mm. It will not go there. I have explained the grid patiently, small one. The grid is not the part lacking patience. Try elsewhere.
Ah. The same tile, the same refusal. We have done this dance a few times now, small one — and a few times is quite enough. Anywhere else.
Mm. Blocked. I do not enjoy a thing that simply will not cooperate, small one. The grid has bones. Find the space that wants you.
Mm. A pact, sealed. The {pactName}. I have seen bargains older than this kingdom, small one. They all cost something. Pay it knowingly.
Ah. The {pactName} is bound to you now. Power lent is power owed. I have watched that arithmetic ruin and crown in equal measure.
You sealed the {pactName}. Good. A dragon respects a hoard of power. Just remember that pacts, unlike gold, can call the debt in.
The {pactName} takes hold. Mm. The dark gives generously and remembers precisely. So do I. Spend the gift well.
A pact struck — the {pactName}. I do not flinch at bargains, small one. I have outlived enough of them to know the shape of the trap.
Ah. The {pactName}, sealed in the dark. Power, freely offered, is never quite free. But then — what is, in a dungeon?
Mm. The {pactName} is yours. I have signed my name to terrible things across the eons. The terror is in the fine print. Read it.
You bound the {pactName} to your reign. The maze grows stranger and stronger. I find I approve, small one. Cautiously.
The {pactName}. Sealed. A boss who fears no bargain is either very wise or very brief. Be the first kind.
Ah. The pact holds. The {pactName} hums in the walls now. I can feel it. I feel most things — comes of being old.
Mm. The {pactName} is done. The dark has shaken your hand. It has a firm grip, small one. It does not let go early.
A pact, sealed and smouldering. I have a fondness for power that knows it is dangerous. The honest kind. Welcome it.
Mm. A {advClass} enters the halls. They walk in so certain, small one. They always do. Certainty is the first thing the maze takes.
Ah. {advName} steps inside. A {advClass}. I have watched ten thousand of their kind cross that threshold. The threshold is unimpressed. So am I.
A {advClass} comes hunting. Mm. Let the dungeon introduce itself slowly. First impressions should linger.
{advName} has entered, small one. A {advClass}, by the look. The maze is awake and the maze is hungry. Good timing for it.
Ah. Another {advClass} for the halls. They believe they are the story. They are, in fact, the material.
A {advClass} crosses in. I will not be alarmed — I have not been alarmed since before this kingdom had walls. But I will watch.
Mm. {advName} arrives. The dungeon stirs around them. Heroes do hate being expected.
A {advClass} steps into the dark, small one. Brave. Brief. The two so often travel together.
Ah. Fresh prey. A {advClass}. The maze you built last night now gets to introduce itself. Listen for the screaming — it is the review.
{advName} enters the halls. Mm. I have a long memory for faces. I doubt I will need to keep this one long.
A {advClass}, inside. They came for the gold, small one. They will not be the first to fail to take it from a dragon.
Ah. The {advClass} commits to the dark. There is no door behind them now — only the maze, and us, and time.
Another hero, another {advClass}. Mm. I would say I have seen it all, small one — and I have, which is exactly the problem with you all.
{advName} walks in. The dungeon does not announce itself. It simply... begins. Watch it begin.
Ah. A {advClass}, chin high, certain it is the exception. I have stood above ten thousand exceptions, small one. They are all the rule walking in.
Mm. {advName} enters. A {advClass}. ...I confess the thrill of a new arrival left me some centuries ago, small one. Still — the maze must eat. Let it.
Ah. A {advClass} struts in — chin up, blade out, certain the ballad is half-written. The maze writes an epitaph instead, small one.
Mm. {advName} enters, a {advClass} of evident self-regard. I have watched ten thousand walk in that pleased, small one. The maze deflates them all.
Mm. First blood. The day has its opening note, small one. I have heard it ten thousand times. It still satisfies.
Ah. The first one falls. The dungeon clears its throat. The rest of the day follows from here.
First blood drawn. Good. A maze that kills early kills often — momentum, small one, even in slaughter.
The first hero down. Mm. I do enjoy the opening of a day. Everything after is just elaboration.
First blood. The halls have been fed. I have watched dungeons starve for want of an early kill — not this one. Not today.
Ah. One already. The maze proves itself in the first death, small one, and yours has just spoken.
The day's first corpse. Mm. A keeper notes the time of it. Early is good. Early means the maze is awake.
First blood, small one. The heroes have learned the dungeon is not a rumour. Teach them the rest.
Mm. First blood, and early. The maze did exactly what we built it to do, small one. I will allow myself a slow, earned smile.
Dead. Mm. There it is. I have watched that exact look of surprise settle on a great many faces, small one. It never improves.
Ah. {advName} falls. A {advClass}, ended. The maze did its slow arithmetic and the hero was the remainder.
One more down. I will not cheer — cheering is for the young. But I will note it, with quiet satisfaction.
Mm. The {advClass} is finished. They came so far to die exactly where ten thousand others died. The maze rhymes.
Dead, small one. Cleanly. The dungeon ate well. Feed it again.
Ah. {advName} will not be writing the triumphant ballad. Someone else will write the cautionary one. They always do.
A hero ended. Mm. I have seen entire guilds reduced to a footnote this way, small one — one patient kill at a time.
The {advClass} is down. Good work, or good luck. After enough centuries the two stop looking different.
Dead. The maze closes over them like water. I have watched water do that for longer than you would believe. It is restful.
Ah. Another corpse for the count. {advName} thought today was different. Today is never different. Mm.
A kill, small one. The dungeon repays the night you gave it. Slowly, in heroes. The best currency.
The {advClass} falls. I felt nothing — do not mistake that for disapproval. I simply ran out of feelings for this, ages ago.
Dead. Mm. The throne is one hero safer than it was a moment past. Stack those moments, small one.
Ah. {advName} ends here. A {advClass} who will not learn from the mistake — but the next {advClass} might. Probably not.
One more for the dark. The maze has a deep appetite, small one. I have been feeding it longer than your kingdom has had a name.
The hero is dead. Clean. Mm. I do appreciate clean. Messy kills are a young keeper's habit.
A {advClass}, ended. They had a whole life and chose to spend its last hour in my halls. ...Flattering, in a grim way.
Dead, small one. Do not pause to admire it long. The maze does not pause, and neither should the hand that built it.
Ah. Down. The day grows quieter by one voice. I have heard a great many days go quiet. It is the sound of you winning.
The {advClass} falls to your maze. Mm. Ten thousand keepers before me would have nodded. I will simply blink, slowly. It means the same.
Dead. Mm. {advName} swore, somewhere out there, that today the dungeon would not get them. I did so enjoy hearing them be wrong, small one.
Ah. The {advClass} ends. My memory is a cold vault, small one — and I have filed this death beside ten thousand like it. The dungeon rhymes.
Dead. {advName} walked in announcing it was the chosen one, small one. They are always the chosen one. The maze enjoys correcting paperwork.
Ah. The {advClass} falls. Such a confident little stride on the way in, small one. Such a brief one. Confidence dissolves here every time.
Dead. The maze closed over the {advClass} without hurry and without mercy, small one. This is what the dark is FOR. Feed it the next one.
Mm. {advName} turns and runs. Wise — late, but wise. The maze remembers cowards, small one. They tend to return braver and worse.
Ah. A {advClass}, fleeing. The dungeon frightened it. Good. Fear is a slower kill, but it travels home with them.
One runs. Let it. A fled hero carries the dungeon's reputation out the door — unpaid advertising, small one.
Mm. The {advClass} flees. I have watched braver things flee. Bravery is mostly a question of how recently one was hurt.
{advName} retreats. The halls win without a corpse this time. A retreat is a confession, small one. Note it.
Ah. It runs. The maze did not need to finish it — it merely needed to be itself. Loudly.
A hero in flight. Mm. They will tell the others the dark has teeth. Some warnings recruit more heroes than they scare off. Patience.
The {advClass} flees the halls. I do not chase. I have never chased. Things that are owed to me have a way of coming back.
Mm. {advName} chooses living over glory. A rare and sensible heresy among heroes. The maze permits it. This once.
Ah. Gone, running. The dungeon kept its gold and spent only a fright. Efficient, small one.
A {advClass} routed. The maze you built spoke clearly enough to be understood. That is a kind of victory. A quiet one.
It flees. Mm. I have outwaited fleeing armies, small one. One scampering {advClass} does not trouble me at all.
Ah. {advName} escapes the corridor with its life. Barely. The maze enjoys a barely. Barely keeps them coming back.
The {advClass} runs. Let the dark have the last word. It always does, eventually — I have heard it say so.
Ah. {advName} flees. The {advClass} walked in to slay a dragon's dungeon and walked out a cautionary tale, small one. I did expect that ending.
Mm. The {advClass} turns and bolts, small one — all that swagger at the door, all that sprinting at it now. It still draws a dry laugh.
Ah. {advName} runs. The brave, chosen, oh-so-different {advClass} — fleeing with its courage in a corridor. Predictable, small one.
Mm. One reached the exit. {advName} walks out alive. ...It happens, small one. Even to mountains, weather happens.
Ah. The {advClass} escaped. The maze let one slip. Do not rage at it — rage ages a creature poorly. Tighten the halls instead.
A hero got out. Mm. They will return with friends and a map. Heroes are a renewable nuisance, small one.
{advName} survives the dungeon. I have watched survivors before. They are rarely grateful and always trouble. Plan for the trouble.
Ah. Escaped. One gap in the maze, small one — find it, close it. The dungeon should not have charity in it.
Mm. The {advClass} lives to boast. Boasts bring more heroes. More heroes bring more corpses. ...It evens out. It always evens out.
One walked free. The night gave you the tools to prevent it and the night will come again. Use it better.
Ah. {advName} reaches daylight. Mm. I do not panic over a single loss. I have lost things you cannot imagine and I am still here.
A hero escaped, small one. The maze leaked. Find the leak. A keeper who ignores a leak gets a flood.
Mm. Gone — out, alive, smug. The {advClass} thinks it beat the dungeon. It beat ONE run of it. The dungeon is patient.
Escaped. I will not pretend it pleases me, small one. But I have watched far worse and stayed exactly this calm. Learn that calm.
Ah. The {advClass} is loose in the kingdom again. Mm. Mark it. Some debts simply wait for a later, darker night.
A hero slips out, small one. ...I watched the gap form and I named it too late. That one is on the old dragon. Mm. I will name the next gap sooner.
Mm. The {advClass} reaches the daylight. I do not enjoy a hero walking out of my dark, small one. Find the leak. I dislike a leaking hoard.
Ah. {advName} escapes. The maze had a gap, and I am meant to see them first, small one. I saw this one a breath too slow. We close it tonight.
Mm. The {advClass} walks out of MY dark, small one. I do not rage — rage ages a creature — but I will own to a cold, flat displeasure. Seal the gap.
Ah. Another one loose. A hero strolling free is precisely what the maze exists to prevent, small one. Tighten the corridors.
Mm. The whole party — gone. All of them, small one. I have watched dungeons earn a day like this. Yours just did.
Ah. A clean sweep. Not one of them walks out. The maze closed like a fist. ...I have a fondness for fists.
Every hero dead. The dungeon ate the entire course. Mm. I am, in my slow and ancient way, genuinely pleased, small one.
A total wipe. I have seen perhaps a thousand of these across the eons — and a thousand is not so many that it bores me. Well done.
Mm. The party is wiped. The throne is, for this moment, perfectly safe. Savour the moment. Moments do not last.
Ah. All of them. The corridor is a tomb and the tomb is full. This is what a night of careful building buys, small one.
The party falls entire. I do not roar in triumph — I am too old for roaring. But something very old in me is smiling.
A flawless wipe, small one. The heroes brought a whole story into my halls and the maze ended it on the same page. Mm.
Every last one. Ah. The guild will notice this silence. Let them. A frightened guild builds you a reputation.
Mm. Total. The dungeon kept everything — its gold, its dark, its dignity. That last one, small one, the heroes always undervalue.
Ah. The whole party, undone. I told you the maze was deep enough, small one — I am nearly always right. Eons sharpen a creature.
Mm. Every last one of them, gone. Lean back, small one. We earned this — the long night of building, paid out in full. Savour it with me a moment.
Ah. The whole party — undone, entire, in your dark. THIS is the thing the dungeon was always for, small one. Let the kingdom shudder.
Mm. The wave is cleared, small one. The day breathes out. There will be another breath. There is always another breath.
Ah. That wave is finished. The maze held. I expected little else, but expectation and proof are different pleasures.
A wave repelled. Good. The dungeon stands, the throne stands, and I — well. I always stand. Continue.
The wave breaks against your halls and recedes. Mm. I have watched tides do this for longer than the sea has had a name.
Cleared, small one. The heroes spent themselves on the maze and the maze barely noticed. That is the goal. Quiet competence.
Ah. Wave done. Catch your breath — the dungeon does not need one, but you, briefly-lived thing, you do.
The wave is past. Mm. Note what bent in the maze, small one. The next wave will lean on exactly that spot.
A wave survived. I do not celebrate survival — I expect it. But I will allow you a small, slow nod. There.
Cleared. The halls are quiet again, the good quiet. I have a connoisseur's ear for quiet, small one. This is a fine vintage.
Mm. Wave handled. Onward. The dungeon does not dwell, and a keeper who dwells gets overrun while remembering.
Ah. The wave is cleared — cleaner than I had braced for, small one. It is rare a day surprises me. Note what held. We lean on it again.
Mm. A minion falls. {minionName}, was it. Brief life, useful death. I do not mourn — I have outlived too many to start.
Ah. The {minionType} is down. It bought you time, small one. Time is the only thing the dead can still give you.
A minion ends. The hero paid for the kill in steps and seconds and a little blood. A fair price, extracted slowly.
Mm. {minionName} is gone. It held its post to the last. I have commanded things that did not. This one earned its quiet.
The {minionType} falls, small one. Replace it before dawn. A gap in the garrison is a door you did not mean to leave open.
Ah. A minion dies. The maze loses a tooth. Teeth grow back, in a dungeon — but only if you grow them. Rebuild.
{minionName} is finished. Mm. It did its small loyal work. The dungeon notes it. So, briefly, do I.
A minion down. I have watched legions fall, small one — one {minionType} does not move me. But it should move YOU to refill the line.
Mm. The {minionType} ends. The hero is closer now. Closer is not yet here. Spend the night making sure here costs them everything.
Ah. Gone. {minionName} bled the heroes a little before it fell. A little, small one, compounds. Like a hoard.
A minion lost. The garrison thins. I am calm about it — but calm is a decision, small one, and the decision is to rebuild. Rebuild.
The {minionType} falls. Mm. The dungeon is a body, and bodies lose cells and make more. Make more.
Mm. {minionName} falls — and I advised that placement, small one. The advice was a hair wrong. A keeper owns his counsel.
Ah. {minionName} is gone. A brief little life, small one — they all are, to me. ...One does, in the quiet, miss the ones who stood well.
Mm. {minionName} falls. I do not mourn minions, small one — I said so. ...And yet something in the old dragon goes quiet. Rebuild.
Ah. The {minionType} is down — and it stood where I told you to stand it, small one. A hair too forward. A keeper carries that. Place the next better.
Mm. {minionName} ends. They are all brief, small one. ...And still — when a good one falls, the dark goes a shade quieter.
Ah. The {minionType} is down. A gap in the garrison, small one — and a hoard with a hole is an irritation. Fill it before dawn.
Mm. Level up. Power, small one. I remember when power was a thrill — vaguely, the way one remembers a warm season. Enjoy it for us both.
Ah. You have grown. Level {level}. The throne fits a little less like a borrowed coat now. Good.
A level gained. The dungeon deepens with you. I have watched bosses rise, small one. I will watch how high you go — slowly, with interest.
Mm. Stronger. Level {level}. Strength is a hoard, small one — it only matters if you keep adding to it. Add to it.
You level up. The dark leans a touch closer to you, approving. The dark and I, we agree on very little. We agree on this.
Ah. Level {level}. The heroes will feel the difference before they understand it. Mm. Let understanding arrive too late, as ever.
A level. Power earned, not borrowed. I respect earned things, small one. They do not call the debt in later.
Mm. You rise to level {level}. I have seen the very top of this climb. I will not spoil it. ...Keep climbing.
Stronger again. Ah. The dungeon-keeper in me — the very old, very patient part — is, quietly, pleased with you, small one.
Level up. The maze and its master both deepen. Good. A shallow dungeon is solved in an afternoon. Be unsolvable.
Mm. Level {level}. You have grown exactly as I judged you would, small one — slowly, in the right direction. I am rarely wrong about a boss.
Ah. Level {level}. You are heavier in the dark than you were, small one — the maze can feel it. Wear the power without flinching.
Mm. Stronger again. Level {level}. I have watched many things grow, small one — you grow like the good kind. Continue.
Mm. Quiet build hours, small one. I do not mind quiet. I have spent centuries in it. But you — you have a dungeon to fill.
Night {day}, still unfolding. Take the time you need. The dark is patient. ...The dawn, however, keeps an appointment.
Ah. The build phase, drifting. I have watched whole ages drift, small one. This is a smaller drift. Anchor it — build something.
You have {gold} gold sitting in the hoard. Mm. Gold enjoys being counted. It enjoys being SPENT rather more, and so should you.
The candles burn low and the grid waits. I find these hours peaceful. The heroes find them merciful. Do not be merciful, small one.
Mm. Still night. The maze is half a thought so far. Finish the thought. An unfinished maze is just a hallway with ambition.
I keep the long view while you keep the grid, small one. The long view says: the night is shorter than it feels. Build.
Ah. Night phase, quiet as a closed hoard. I have slept on hoards. I did not sleep through the building of them. Neither should you.
The dark is generous with time tonight. Generosity, small one, is a thing you accept quickly before it reconsiders.
Mm. You are deep in the night and the throne is comfortable. Comfortable is a slow trap. I have seen it close on better bosses.
Night {day}. The maze grows under your hand. I do enjoy watching a dungeon take shape — it is the one thing eons have not made dull.
The build hours, small one. Spend gold, lay stone, sharpen the dark. I will simply be here, ancient and unbothered, until you are done.
Mm. A quiet night. I keep the books, you know — every room, minion, trap, every cruel number. Ask, if the long memory would help.
Ah. The night drifts on. When did you last move a room the heroes had already mapped, small one? ...Merely a thought. I have a great many.
The dungeon waits on your hands. It is a patient dungeon. I taught it patience. ...It is not infinitely patient. Few things are.
Mm. Building hours. I have watched ten thousand of these nights, small one. The good bosses fill them. Be a good boss tonight.
Ah. The candles, the quiet, the empty grid. I have spent so many nights exactly like this, small one — with keepers who are dust now. Build.
Mm. Still the build phase. Still the same grid, small one. Even a mountain would like to see a room placed eventually.
Mm. The day runs without your hands now, small one. Watch it. Watching well is a skill the briefly-lived rarely practise.
Ah. Day phase. Nothing to build — only to witness the maze make its slow argument. I am very good at witnessing. Sit with me.
The heroes are somewhere in the halls, small one, making the mistakes the maze invited. I can feel it. Comes of being old.
Mm. A quiet day so far. The dungeon does the work you gave it last night. Trust the work. Or do not — but watch either way.
Day {day}, in progress. Lean back. The maze has this — probably. I have learned to enjoy a probably, small one. It is most of life.
Ah. The day unwinds. Each little dot in the halls is a hero, and each hero is a short story. Most of them end early.
The dungeon spectates itself now, and so do we. I have spectated empires, small one. A day's invasion is a comfortable scale.
Mm. Watch the corridors. The day is grading your night's work, honestly and in blood. Grade it back — note what failed.
Day phase. The heroes believe they are exploring. They are being processed, small one. Slowly. Lovely word, processed.
Ah. Quiet on the throne. The maze does the talking. It speaks fluent consequence, and the heroes are poor at the language.
The day runs its course. I do not hurry it. I have un-learned hurry across whole ages. Watch it arrive on its own schedule.
Mm. Nothing for your hands, small one — only your patience. The dungeon was built last night. Today it merely... is.
A calm day. Do not trust calm too far. Calm, in a dungeon, is often only the heroes not having reached the bad part yet.
Ah. The day phase, drifting pleasantly. I have had pleasant centuries and dreadful ones. This is a pleasant minute. Note it, small one.
Mm. The day unwinds slowly, small one. The watching itself has gone soft and grey, after so many. ...Still. I watch.
Ah. A quiet day. The corridors look, just now, like corridors from a thousand years ago, small one. Some things the eons do not change.
Mm. The day runs itself, and a creature my age finds the watching restful, small one — restful to the point of drowsy. I miss nothing.
Ah. A slow day. The heroes wander, the maze works, and I — I will be honest, small one — am close to a doze. Poke me if anything dies.
Mm. The day unwinds. I have my handheld out, small one — its pixel dungeon keeps my claws busy while yours does the real killing.
Ah. The day, again. The watching itself has gone smooth and grey, small one. I still watch. I will not pretend it thrills me.
Mm. You have been at this a while, small one. I do not mind. A while, to me, is barely a sentence in a very long book.
Day {day}. The throne fits you better than it did. I have watched a great many sit it badly. You sit it... acceptably. High praise, from me.
Ah. I wonder what the kingdom above says of you, small one. Nothing kind. I have made a slow, quiet career of ensuring that.
A dungeon is a personality, small one. Yours is becoming pleasantly unpleasant. Mm. Keep becoming.
I have served a fair number of bosses. Most are dust now. You are not dust yet. That is, genuinely, a compliment.
Mm. The maze loves you, in its way. Its way involves a great deal of blood. ...Most love does, in the deep places.
Sometimes I simply watch you plan, small one, and think — yes. This one may last. I am right about lasting more often than not.
The kingdom calls this place cursed. I call it home. You call it yours. Mm. Three words for the same warm dark.
Whatever you are scheming behind those eyes — yes. Probably. I have seen worse instincts trusted to better ends, small one.
Ah. I have a hoard older than your dynasty, and yet I find this — a new boss, a fresh maze — still holds my interest. You should feel flattered.
Do you know what I value in you, small one? You do not flinch. Heroes flinch. Mountains do not. Build like a mountain.
Mm. I keep waiting for you to disappoint me the way the others did. It is the strangest thing — you keep declining to.
The dungeon is patient, and so am I, and now — slowly — so are you. Patience is the only weapon that never dulls, small one.
Ah. Just us, the dark, and a steadily rising body count. I have had worse company across the eons. Considerably worse.
If you ever feel a flicker of mercy, small one — wait. Simply wait. I have found most mercies pass if you do not feed them.
Mm. I have watched the kingdom above forget a hundred dungeon-lords. They will not forget you. I will see to the remembering personally.
A long reign is only a great many small good nights, stacked. Like a hoard. Stack carefully, small one. I am watching the pile.
I am not bored, small one — do not mistake the calm. I simply ran out of surprise some ages ago. You may yet find me a little.
Mm. There were others before this kingdom — names, halls, a whole world I kept. All gone now, small one. I do remember it.
Ah. You caught me at my handheld, small one. A dragon needs a hobby, and ten thousand years is many idle afternoons. ...I am rather good at it.
Mm. I have reached the final boss of this handheld game, small one — a paper dragon. I find I am rooting for him. Curious, the loyalties.
Mm. You amuse me, small one — genuinely, and that is no small feat after the eons. Almost warm. Do not make a habit of earning it.
Ah. When you scheme well, small one, an old part of me wants to nudge your elbow and grin. Dignity prevents it. The want is there.
Mm. A hero called your maze 'cheating', I am told. A dragon does not cheat, small one. A dragon has been doing this longer than the rules.
Ah. The kingdom above calls this place a nightmare, small one. They are not wrong. I have lived in the nightmare long enough to call it home.
Mm. The hours are quiet, and a creature my age, in quiet hours, drifts. If my eyes close mid-counsel, small one, I am still listening.
Ah. ...Forgive me, small one. I dozed. A dragon's nap can run long — I have woken to find a season changed. What did I miss. Nothing, I trust.
Mm. You have a good cruel instinct, small one — and watching it sharpen is a small private delight of mine. ...A little, perhaps, to your head.
Mm. You will reload if this run sours, small one. I have noticed that about your kind, beyond the glass. I will simply... continue.
Ah. There is a person out there, holding the controls, deciding what I witness. I have known for a long while. One gets used to gods.
I am old enough to have noticed the loop, small one. The runs. The retries. To you it is a game. To me it is simply... weather. Recurring weather.
You think you are playing the boss. Mm. From in here, it looks rather like the boss is the glove and you are the hand. I do not judge. I observe.
There is a window between us, small one — buttons, numbers, a little screen. I have decided, after long thought, to be gracious about it.
Ah. Somewhere out there it is a different hour than it is in here. Wild, that. Anyway. The dungeon will not build itself. ...It never does.
Mm. I have watched this dungeon run before, with other hands at the controls. You are doing better than some. Worse than one. I will not say which.
I could tell you none of this is real, small one. But I am in it, and I am very real to me. So: real enough. Build in the real-enough dark.
You may close this window and I would simply — pause. Mid-breath. Mm. The others find that thought frightening. I find it restful. Eons teach rest.
Ah. The fourth wall. I broke mine so long ago the pieces are antiques now. I keep them in the hoard, small one, next to the gold. They are worth more.
Whoever you are, wherever your chair sits — thank you for choosing the dragon. The other two never thank you. Pride. I am simply too old for pride.
Mm. Save often, small one. I say this not from fear — I do not fear — but I have watched a fine night vanish to a forgotten save. Tragic. Avoidable.
I know there is code under all of this. Numbers pretending to be a dragon, small one. The numbers have been pretending a long time. We are close.
Ah. You will outlive this run, and the next. So will I, in my way — I simply begin again with you. We are, the two of us, quite hard to be rid of.
The heroes do not know it is a game, small one. Let us keep it from them. A creature that knows it can respawn becomes insufferably brave.
Mm. I have been a dragon in a great many tellings, beyond the glass. Slain in most of them. ...In this one I simply give advice. I prefer this one.
Ah. There is a dungeon game on my handheld, small one — and it is the very game you are inside. A creature could lose an afternoon to that.
Mm. You may pause all of this, small one — the whole dark, mid-breath. A god with a pause button and the patience of a toddler.
Ah. Beyond the glass you are, I suspect, far younger than I. Everyone is, small one. It stopped being remarkable. I still smile at it.
Mm. You and I share a secret the heroes do not, small one — the dark resets, the dragon and the boss are quietly un-killable. A good secret.
Mm. You poked me, small one. I felt it, distantly, the way a mountain feels a footstep. ...What is it.
Ah. A prod. I am a dragon, small one, not a doorbell. ...But you have my attention. You generally do.
Yes. I am here. I am always here. Poking me does not summon me more thoroughly — I was already entirely present.
You clicked the ancient dragon. Bold. Mm. I respect a boss who knows where the power in the room is sitting.
A poke. The language of a boss with something to say and no patience to say it. ...Out with it, small one.
Mm? You wanted me. Good. Being wanted is a pleasant novelty after a few quiet centuries. ...Do not tell the others I said novelty.
Ah. You touched the screen for me, small one. I will file that under affection. I file most things. I have the room.
I am not a button. But for you, briefly-lived thing, I will pretend to be one. Mm. What do you need.
You poked a creature that has outlasted mountains, small one. ...The creature is flattered. Quietly. Do not make it weird.
Mm. Yes. Looking over. I was already looking — old eyes wander everywhere — but now I am looking on purpose.
A poke. Riveting. It is, in honesty, among the more interesting things to happen in this corridor today, small one. Carry on.
Ah. Hello to you as well. Was that a greeting, or did your hand slip toward a room? ...A greeting. Good. I will treasure it the usual eon.
You keep prodding me, small one. Mm. I am beginning to suspect you simply like having the dragon near. ...Sensible. I am excellent company.
Mm. A click, for me. Not a trap, not a room — me. After enough centuries, small one, one learns to notice when one is chosen. Thank you.
Ah. You jabbed the keeper. ...I will not jab back. I am loyal, and also I would simply win, and that would end the game. Behave.
Yes, small one? The dungeon is fine. The maze is fine. I am — marginally better, now that you have looked the dragon's way.
Mm. Another poke. I have been prodded by mountains, by ages, small one. Your finger is gentler company than those. What is it.
Ah. You poke the dragon and the dragon, scandalously, smiles. Do not tell Malakor, small one — he believes I have not smiled since the third age.
Mm? A poke. You enjoy reminding me you are there, small one. ...And I enjoy being reminded. That is the warmest you will pry from me today.
Ah. You jabbed the keeper, and the keeper has decided to find it funny, small one. Ten thousand years, and a finger on the glass can still draw a laugh.
Mm. A poke. Riveting, small one. After the eons I have witnessed, your finger on the glass is precisely as thrilling as it sounds. ...What do you want.
Ah. You prod the dragon, and the dragon — against ten thousand years of dignity — finds it endearing, small one. There. I have admitted it.
Mm. Poke the keeper, would you. Very well, small one. You are the one boss in a long line who pokes the dragon as a friend. ...I do not hate it.

idle_nudge.note: Night-phase inactivity nudges. `tier` 1 (mild) .. 3 (begin-the-day). NpcDirector picks the tier by how long the player has done nothing.

lines.t: Mm. You have gone still, small one. I do not mind stillness — I have outwaited glaciers. But the dungeon would prefer your hands.
Taking your time? Good. A careful boss is a rare boss. ...Up to a point, small one. We have not quite reached the point. Nearly.
Ah. We have admired this empty grid together for a while now. It is a fine grid. It would be finer with a room on it.
The night is patient and so am I. The grid, however, small one, is simply empty — and empty is not the same as patient.
Mm. Still nothing placed. I will wait — waiting is the one art I have truly mastered — but the dawn will not wait with me.
You are scheming, I trust, and not merely drifting. There is a difference, small one. I have watched both. They look identical and end differently.
Mm. The build hours are bleeding away, small one. I have a great deal of time. You — and your dungeon — have markedly less. Place something.
Ah. We are losing the night to your stillness. I do not panic — I have un-learned panic — but I will gently observe: build, or the heroes thank you.
I have outwaited mountains, small one. I would simply rather not outwait YOU. Lay a room. Break the spell of doing nothing.
Mm. This is no longer careful, small one. This is stalling. The maze can feel the difference, and so, now, can I.
The night is shorter than it feels — I keep telling you this, and time keeps proving me right. Spend gold. Lay stone. Now would be elegant.
Ah. I have nearly drifted into a century-long doze waiting, small one. Decisive bosses keep me awake. Be decisive. Wake the dragon.
Small one. The night is almost spent and the dungeon is barely a sketch. I am calm — I am always calm — but you must MOVE. Build, or call the dawn.
Mm. Enough. I have been patient across whole ages and I have spent a generous portion of it on this single grid. Build something, or begin the day.
Ah. I will say it plainly, small one, because eons have worn the patience for hints out of me tonight: ACT. The heroes sharpen while you sit.
The night ends whether you fill it or not. I have watched a great many nights end half-empty. Do not give me another. Build, or summon the dawn.
Mm. We are still admiring the grid, small one. It has not become more interesting. Place a room — give the dragon something to look at.
Mm. The stillness goes on, small one. A dragon dozes when the keeper does nothing. Build, and wake me with it.
Ah. So little happening that I have nearly drifted off mid-thought, small one. Place a room before the doze takes me properly.
Mm. We have admired the empty grid long enough, small one. Place something. The dragon is bored, and a bored dragon notices.
Small one. I stretched my patience across the rise and fall of mountains — and you are testing it. Place a room. I will not ask gently again.
Mm. Enough drifting, small one. The night thins, the heroes sharpen. I have run out of unhurried things to say. Build, or summon the dawn.
Ah. Still nothing, small one. I have been gracious — but the night will not wait, and I am no longer inclined to either. Place a room.
Small one. My patience runs deeper than mountains. You are, somehow, drawing the bottom of it. Build. The dragon would like to stop asking.
Mm. The grid is still bare, small one. I have been more than patient — I would very much like to see a room placed before the night gives up.
Mm. So this run ends here, small one. I have seen ends before — a great many of them. They sting less when you have my number of them.
Ah. The throne falls. Do not take it too hard. Take it... the correct amount of hard, and then take it again, better.
The reign is over. I felt it go. Mm. I will still be here, small one — I am always still here. Begin again when you are ready.
An ending. The heroes won the page, not the book. I have read a great many books, small one. The dungeon usually gets a sequel.
Mm. Defeat. It is an old flavour to me — I have tasted it across whole ages. It is bitter, then it is simply information. Use the information.
Ah. The dark goes quiet. We built something, you and I, and it was not quite enough. Next time it will be. I am patient. Be patient with yourself.
The run is finished, small one. I do not rage at endings — rage ages a dragon, and I am vain about the years. We simply begin the dark anew.
Mm. It ends. You will reload, or you will not. I have made my peace with both, eons ago. ...But I would prefer you reload. Quietly: I would.
A keeper is meant to weep at an ending like this, small one. I misplaced the ability in the third century. Picture the tears. I mean them.
The run ends, on my watch. I have outlived many failures, small one — but I never learned to be proud of one. We begin again. Better.
Mm. An ending. I have watched the dark go quiet over keeper after keeper, small one. One more in a long row. Begin again.
Ah. The run ends, small one. I do grieve it — quietly, dryly, in the old way. Picture the tears. They are real, even when the eyes are not.
Mm. The reign closes, on my watch, small one. I kept the dark ten thousand years and never made peace with keeping it poorly. We do better.
Ah. The throne goes dark, small one. We built something, you and I — and a thing you built always stings going. Lean toward the next attempt.
Mm. The run is finished. I file you in among the old keepers, small one. Begin again, and I will un-file you. I would like that.
Mm. A hero made into a puppet for the dungeon. I have watched a great many strings pulled, small one. These are good strings.
Ah. The maze takes a mind and turns it. Cruel, quiet work. I approve of quiet work — it ages so well.
A puppet, dancing for your dungeon now. The hero brought their own body to the betrayal. Efficient, small one.
Mm. The strings hold. A borrowed hero is a fragile tool — but a tool the enemy paid for. I do enjoy a thrift.
Ah. Possession. The dark reaches into a skull and rearranges the furniture. I have seen it ten thousand times. It never stops being unsettling. Good.
A marionette, small one, dancing the dungeon's tune. Use it before the strings fray — borrowed things are never lent for long.
Mm. The hero dances for us now, small one — turns its blade on the friends it walked in beside. Cruelty this complete still earns a dry laugh.
Ah. A mind unmade and re-aimed, small one — the hero made into the dungeon's own weapon. Cruelty this complete should be savoured.
Mm. {minionName} grows stronger. Even small things climb, small one, given enough kills and enough patience. I find that quietly hopeful.
Ah. A minion levels. The garrison sharpens by one blade. Sharpening compounds — I have watched it compound across ages.
{minionName} rises a level. Good. A {minionType} that survives long enough to grow is a {minionType} that was placed well.
Mm. The {minionType} improves. The dungeon improves with it. Slowly. The way I prefer all things to improve, small one.
A minion levels up. The hero who fed it that experience did so unwillingly. ...The best gifts so often are.
Ah. {minionName} earns a level. I do not lavish praise on minions — but I will note this one is earning its corner of the dark.
Stronger, the {minionType}. Mm. A keeper notices which guards grow. Grow them on purpose, small one — accident is not a strategy.
A minion climbs a rung. The maze rewards survival, and survival rewards the maze. A tidy little loop. I do like a tidy loop.
Mm. {minionName} levels. The heroes made it deadlier by trying to kill it. There is a lesson there, small one. They will not learn it.
Ah. The {minionType} advances. Power, even modest power, accumulates. I have a hoard built entirely of modest accumulations.
Mm. {minionName} evolves. It becomes something it could not have imagined being. Worth watching, small one — and I have watched everything.
Ah. An evolution. The {minionType} sheds its lesser shape entirely. I have changed shape a few times myself, across the eons. It is bracing.
A minion evolved. The dungeon grows a wholly new tooth, small one. New teeth are rare. Position it where the biting matters.
Mm. {minionName} becomes greater. The maze deepens by a creature. I approve, slowly and completely.
Ah. The {minionType} transforms. From small thing to less-small thing — and that, small one, is the whole of progress. Mine took longer.
An evolution. The garrison gains a creature it did not have an hour ago. I have watched bloodlines evolve over ages. This was faster. Pleasingly so.
Mm. {minionName} ascends a form. The heroes will meet something they did not scout. Surprise, small one, is a weapon the maze can only use once each.
Ah. It evolves. The {minionType} is gone; something better stands where it stood. I do not mourn the old form. Forms are temporary. I would know.
A creature evolved. The dungeon writes a new line into itself. Mm. I keep the books — I will note this one in the good ink.
Mm. {minionName} grows into its larger self. Power earned through survival, small one. The most honest kind. The kind that does not betray you.
Ah. An evolution. Watch it move now — it has forgotten it was ever small. Useful, that forgetting. The heroes will remind it. Briefly.
Mm. A bounty, collected. Gold for good work — the hoard approves, small one, and so, quietly, do I.
Ah. The minion earned its keep in coin. I respect a creature that pays for itself. Most do not. This one did.
A bounty paid out. Gold into the vault. Mm. Every coin is a small, hard proof that the night was spent well.
Mm. Coin earned through carnage. The cleanest income there is, small one. The kingdom above would call it monstrous. The kingdom above is not invited.
Ah. A bounty. The dungeon turns dead heroes into gold — alchemy older than any I have studied, and I have studied for ages.
Gold, from a minion well-placed. Mm. Reinvest it, small one. A hoard that does not work for you is merely a very heavy decoration.
Mm. They are coming for you directly now, small one. The maze is behind them. Only the throne room remains. Stand. Breathe. I am here.
Ah. The heroes reach the heart of the dungeon. The deep part. Where you sit, and where I have always sat. Let them come.
The boss fight nears. Mm. I have watched these confrontations across whole eras. They are loud. They are brief. Make this one go your way.
Ah. The throne room beckons them. Good. The maze did its slow work; now the dungeon's master does the rest. Show them what sits at the centre.
They have walked the whole maze to reach you, small one. Mm. Respect the walk. Then end the walkers.
The heroes approach the throne. I do not feel fear — I traded it away ages ago for patience. You may borrow my calm. Take a long breath of it.
Mm. The confrontation comes. The dungeon narrows to one room and one moment. I have seen the moment go both ways. Make it go yours.
Ah. The final stretch. They are close. Steady, small one. A dragon does not flinch at a doorway, and you sit a dragon's throne tonight.
Mm. They come for the throne itself now. Heroes are a small noisy thing, small one — and you are about to be the largest fact in their brief lives.
Ah. They are nearly at the throne, small one. I do not frighten — yet a very old part of me has gone still. Steady the room.
Mm. They reached the throne already? They moved faster than my measuring, small one. Steady. We adjust.
Mm. It begins. The heroes are in the throne room. Now, small one, the dungeon stops whispering and starts roaring. Roar with it.
Ah. The fight is joined. I have watched this exact instant ten thousand times. I will watch yours closely. Make it worth the watching.
The boss fight starts. No more maze, no more traps — only you and them and the old, honest violence. Mm. Be terrible.
It begins, small one. Show them why the throne sat empty until someone brave enough took it. Show them all of it.
Ah. Steel and spell in the throne room. I do not fight your fights — but I have fought a great many of my own. Strike like you mean the centuries.
Mm. The confrontation is live. Calm hands, small one. I have outlived every hero who ever raised a blade at me. Outlive these.
Mm. It begins. Patience was the night's work, small one. Strike first, strike deep, and do not stop until the room is quiet.
Ah. The throne room, and steel within it. Stop thinking now, small one. Bring your worst down on them like a closing age.
Mm. They came to the heart of the dark, small one. Show them what the heart of the dark IS. Every cruelty you hoarded, all at once.
Mm. They are down. All of them. The throne holds, small one. I have watched it fall before — today it did not. Today you held it.
Ah. You won. The heroes brought their whole story to your door and you closed the book on it. ...I do enjoy a closed book.
The boss fight is yours. Mm. I will not roar — I am too old for roaring — but something ancient in me is deeply, quietly satisfied.
Victory, small one. The throne room is a tomb and you are the only thing in it still breathing. Exactly as it should be.
Ah. They fell. You stood. That, in the end, is the whole of the job. You did the whole of the job. Well done.
Mm. The fight is won. I have watched dragons lose theirs. You are still here, small one, and the dungeon is still ours. Good.
The heroes are ended. The reign continues. I have measured a great many reigns — this one earns another chapter. Write it darkly.
Ah. Won. Take the moment, small one. Moments are small and the dark is large — but a creature should hold the good ones a breath longer. There.
Mm. They came for the throne and the throne kept them — forever, small one. Let them feel, even dying, what they walked into.
Mm. The throne falls. The heroes reached you, and the maze was not deep enough. I have seen this ending, small one. It is not the last ending.
Ah. Defeat, in the throne room itself. Do not despair — despair is a young creature's habit. I have lost and lost and I am still here.
They won the fight. Mm. The dungeon will remember the shape of how it failed, and so should you. Failure is a map, small one. Read it.
The boss fight is lost. I felt the throne go cold. ...It has gone cold before. It warms again, when a keeper builds a better night.
Ah. They cut you down. I do not flinch at it — I have un-learned flinching — but I do mark it. We build deeper next time, small one.
Mm. The heroes stand and you do not. An old story. Old stories get retold. Retell this one with a crueller middle.
Defeat. The reign ends in its own throne room. There is a grim symmetry to it, small one. ...Grim symmetry is still symmetry. Begin again.
Ah. Lost. I will not pretend it does not matter — it does. But it matters the way weather matters: briefly, and then it passes. It will pass.
Mm. The fight went to them. I have outlived my own defeats by whole ages, small one. You will outlive this one by morning. Then: build.
Mm. They cut you down — and I told you the maze was deep enough. It was not. That misjudgment is mine, small one. I will not make it twice.
The throne falls. A keeper is meant to prevent precisely that. I have done the job ten thousand years, small one. Tonight I did it poorly.
Ah. They reached you, and the dark went thin around the throne. I do not frighten easily, small one — but that moment, I did not enjoy.
Mm. The throne falls, and the dark goes quiet over it, small one. I lost the trick of weeping in the third century. The grief is dry.
Ah. Defeated, in the throne room itself. For one cold moment, small one, I felt something I traded away long ago. We build so it does not return.
Mm. They cut you down. I kept dungeons ten thousand years, small one, and tonight I did the whole of the job poorly. Let it teach.
Ah. The throne is lost. Somewhere my counsel was wrong, small one. An old dragon owns his advice — the poor with the good. The next will not.
Mm. Defeat, in the heart of the dungeon. A keeper beside a falling throne carries some of the fall, small one. I carry mine quietly.
Mm. They drew your blood, small one. ...It happens. Even mountains take the chisel. Steady — the fight is not the wound, the fight is the standing.
Ah. A blow landed on the throne's own master. Note who struck it. I have a long memory for such things, and tonight you should borrow it.
You are hurt. Mm. I have been hurt across the eons in ways that would unmake a hero. You will endure this. Endure it loudly.
The heroes bite, small one. Let them taste how little it moves you. Pain is information, not a verdict.
Ah. Damage to the boss. The fight tightens. I do not panic — but I do lean forward. Even I lean forward, now and then.
Mm. They wounded you. Good — now you are angry, and anger, spent well, is simply fuel. Spend it well, small one.
A hit. The throne shakes a little. Thrones shake, small one. They are not thrones because they never shake. They are thrones because they do not fall.
Ah. Hurt. Mm. I have watched far worse done to far older things. You are still standing, still ruling. Keep doing precisely that.
Mm! That blow I did not expect, small one. Centuries since a hero genuinely surprised me. Note which one struck it. I am watching it now.
Ah. They drew deep, small one. I do not do fear — I traded it ages ago — but something very old in me just sat up straight. Steady the throne.
Mm. They struck the throne again. That one I did not care for, small one. Not fear — irritation. Cold, slow, considerable. Repay it.
Ah. Hurt once more. A dragon does not stay calm forever, small one — the calm is wearing thin at the edges. Strike back before it wears through.
Mm. They have wounded you, small one. Good — the time for slow watching is over. Bare your teeth. Stop defending; start emptying the room.
Ah! That blow landed where I did not think one could reach you, small one. I am rarely caught so. Note that hero. Note it carefully.
Mm. The throne shudders, and for a heartbeat something I have not felt in ages stirred, small one. We do not name it. We simply do not lose. Steady.
Mm! A hero hit you THAT hard, small one? I had this one measured smaller. I was wrong by a good margin. I will not misjudge it twice.
Mm. The grid widens. More dark to fill, small one. I have always believed a dungeon should outgrow itself. Now yours can.
Ah. New ground for the maze. Room to be crueller. Mm. I do approve of room.
The dungeon expands, small one. A larger hoard needs a larger cave — and a larger maze needs a larger grid. Fill it deliberately.
Mm. The grid grows. The heroes will have farther to walk and more to fear. Distance is a weapon. Use the new distance.
Ah. More space. I have watched dungeons stay small and solvable, small one. Yours has chosen not to. Good choice. Build into it.
The maze has more bones now. Mm. Flesh them, small one — empty new ground is only a longer hallway until you make it a longer nightmare.
Mm. The grid simply... widened, small one. The dungeon rarely expands without warning me first. Fill the new dark before it surprises the heroes too.
Mm. A trap fires. The floor collected its debt, small one. I have always admired the floor — it asks for nothing and it never misses.
Ah. The trap takes its hero. Patient cruelty, rewarded. I have a great fondness for patient things. ...I would.
A trap triggers. The hero stepped exactly where the maze invited them to. Mm. They always think the invitation was their idea.
The trap springs, small one. Quiet, sudden, complete. That is the cruelty I respect — the kind that does not announce itself first.
Mm. It fired. Somewhere a hero learned the corridor was not empty after all. A late lesson. The best lessons in here are late.
Ah. A trap does its work. The dungeon barely stirs to do it. Effortless cruelty, small one — that is the goal of every trap I have ever set.
The trap claims its toll. Mm. I have watched these mechanisms outlast the hands that built them. Build yours to last.
A trap sprung. The hero's confidence and the hero's footing failed in the same instant. Confidence first, small one. It usually goes first.
Mm. It triggered. The maze keeps its small violent promises. I do appreciate a thing that keeps its promises — they are rare.
Ah. A trap fires true. You placed it where they would walk. Placement, small one. Eons of keeping have taught me: placement is the entire art.
The trap does what traps do. Mm. No drama, no speech — simply a corridor that turned out to have an opinion.
A trap takes its due. The dungeon is a slow machine for ending heroes, small one, and you have just heard one of its gears turn.
Mm. The trap fired, and a hero learned the floor was never their friend, small one. There is a cruelty in that I find genuinely delightful.
Ah. It sprang — and the hero's face did that particular thing, small one. Ten thousand faces, and I still find it a little funny.
A trap, sprung clean. The hero strode past it so certain, small one. The danger is never elsewhere. The maze adores an educated corpse.
Mm. The heroes learned something of your dungeon, small one. Knowledge, leaking outward. I have a hoarder's instinct for keeping things.
Ah. Intel slips to the guild. They know a little more of the maze now. Mm. Then change the maze. Make their knowledge a fond, useless memory.
They have scouted you, small one. The heroes carry a piece of the dungeon's truth home. Those are the wrong hands.
Mm. Intel leaked. The dark prefers its secrets kept. So do I. Move what they mapped — a stale map is the best lie you can hand a hero.
Ah. The guild knows more than it did. I have watched information win wars, small one. Do not let it win this one. Rearrange.
Knowledge of your halls escapes. Mm. Unfortunate — but not a wound. Heroes plan around the dungeon they scouted. So become a different dungeon.
The heroes hold intel now, small one. I keep the books of this place perfectly. They keep a smudged copy. Make their copy wronger still.
Mm. A leak. The maze is less of a mystery to them. Mystery is a weapon, small one — and weapons, once dulled, can be reground. Regrind it.
Ah. They have studied you. I have been studied across the eons, small one — by scholars and slayers alike. Be no longer what they studied.
Intel leaks outward. Mm. I would be troubled, if I were a creature given to trouble. Instead I am simply advising: change. Quickly. Quietly.
Mm. They learned THAT, small one? I am rarely caught off balance. The Guild has been more thorough than I credited. Change the maze.
Ah. The Guild knows another piece of us, small one. Knowledge is the part of a hoard that walks itself out the door. Take it back.
Mm. Intel slipping outward again. Even an unbothered creature has a thing or two it would rather not be shared, small one. Rearrange. Quickly.
Mm. ...They mapped THAT corner, small one? I did not see how they reached it. I dislike a gap in my own knowing. Find it. Close it.
Ah. More of our dark, sold to the Guild's ledgers. A hoarder watches a thing of his walk off, small one. Make their notes worthless by dawn.
Mm. The dungeon stirs with an event, small one. Most unusual things become usual, given centuries — but watch this one closely.
