Ah. An event in the halls. The night turns strange. Strange is not the same as bad, small one — often it is opportunity wearing an odd hat.
The dungeon throws a complication at you. Mm. I have weathered complications older than empires. Read this one, then bend it your way.
An event, small one. The maze refuses to be predictable. Good — a predictable dungeon is a solved dungeon. Let it surprise even me a little.
Mm. Something is happening. The dark has its moods, and tonight it is in one. Move with it, not against it — the dark does not lose arguments.
Ah. An event takes the dungeon by the collar. I have watched the world do this for ages, small one. Steady hands. Strange nights reward steady hands.
The night develops a wrinkle. Mm. I do enjoy a wrinkle — it has been some centuries since anything genuinely caught me off guard. Perhaps this will.
An event, small one. Opportunity and hazard, twisted together as they always are. Pull the right thread. I will tell you if you pull the wrong one.
Mm. The dungeon does something the dungeon does not usually do. Note it. The unusual, handled well, is how a keeper writes a memorable night.
Ah. The halls have an announcement. I have heard a great many across the eons, small one. Most were nothing. Attend this one, just in case.
Mm. ...Well. THAT, small one, I have not seen before — and I have, with very few exceptions, seen everything. Attend this one closely. So shall I.
Mm. The dungeon does something, and I — I will admit it — am simply staring, small one. It has been long since the dark caught me flat. Watch.
Ah. An event. Very little of the long grind of ages stops a thought of mine mid-sentence, small one. This did. Attend it closely.
Mm. The dungeon just did THAT, small one? That was not in my books — and I keep them down to the last cruel number. The dark surprised its keeper.
Mm. A bounty hunter comes for you, small one. Not a hero — a professional. Professionals do not flinch at a maze. They invoice it.
Ah. Someone has put a price on the throne. Flattering, in its way. I have had prices on my head across whole ages. None of those hunters collected.
A hunter approaches — cold, paid, and uninterested in glory. Mm. Glory makes heroes careless. Coin makes hunters careful. Be ready for careful.
The bounty hunter nears the halls, small one. They came for your head as a transaction. Make the transaction... unprofitable.
Mm. A contract walks toward your dungeon. I have outlived the parties to a thousand, small one. But respect this one. Hunters earn their fee.
Ah. A hunter, incoming. They will not be impressed by the maze, small one — only slowed by it. Slowing a professional is still winning. Slow them.
Mm. Heroes incoming, small one. The kingdom keeps sending them. It has done so for as long as there have been dungeons. It is almost touching.
Ah. A fresh wave approaches the halls. Steady. I have watched the tide come in ten thousand times — the tide has never once won. Let it break on you.
More heroes near the dungeon. Mm. Brave, certain, doomed in roughly that order. The maze is awake. Let them meet it.
The next wave comes, small one. Brace the halls. I do not brace — I have nothing left that braces — but you should. Mind the corridors.
Mm. Heroes on the approach. The guild never tires of feeding the dark. Generous of them. Receive the gift properly.
Ah. They are coming. I can feel the tread of them, small one — old ears, long practice. The dark is hungry and the kingdom is catering.
A wave approaches. Mm. Each of them believes they will be the one to reach the hoard. Each of them is mistaken. Confirm it for them.
Heroes incoming, small one. The maze you built is about to be graded again. I have read a great many of its report cards. Earn a cruel one.
Ah. The kingdom sends more children with swords. I watched their grandparents fail in these same halls, small one. Lineage does not improve them.
Mm. The knowledge of the dungeon, laid out. I keep it all in my head, small one — eons of room for it — but a written copy never hurt a careful boss.
Ah. Studying what you know. Good. I have always held that knowing the board is half of winning it. The other half is nerve. You supply the nerve.
The knowledge screen, small one. Every truth the heroes have pried loose, gathered in one place. Know what they know. Then make it obsolete.
Mm. Reviewing the intel. A keeper who reads is a keeper who lasts. I have read for a very long time. It shows, I am told.
Ah. The knowledge ledger. Mm. The heroes scout you; you scout yourself harder. Stay one chapter ahead of their map, small one.
You study what is known. Good. I have watched bosses ignore this screen and lose for the ignoring. Do not be a cautionary tale.
Mm. The knowledge of the halls. Information is a hoard too, small one — and like any hoard, it is only useful if you actually visit it.
Ah. Reviewing the deep facts of the dungeon. Take your time. The page does not age. ...I do, technically, but very, very slowly.
Mm. Looking over yourself, small one — the boss you are. Wise. A keeper should know what sits the throne. I have studied many of you.
Ah. The boss panel. Your abilities, your shape, your edge. Know your own teeth before you ask them to bite.
You inspect your own power, small one. Good. I have watched dragons forget their own strengths. Embarrassing, that. Do not be a dragon about it.
Mm. The measure of the boss. Every reign is built on what its master can do. Read your own measure carefully.
Ah. Studying the throne's occupant. I approve. The most dangerous thing in this dungeon should be the thing you understand best — and that is you.
The boss screen, small one. Mm. Your signature ability is a tool with one perfect moment. Learn the moment. Waste nothing.
Mm. You weigh your own strength. I have weighed it too, quietly, since you took the seat. ...You are heavier than you look, small one. Use that.
Ah. Know thyself, and so on. Ancient advice — I am ancient, so I am allowed to give it. Know what you are. Then be it, without apology.
Mm. The boss panel, small one. You stand above every other thing here. Carry yourself like it. The throne can tell when its master forgets.
Mm. The intel on the heroes, small one. Know the prey. I have hunted across whole ages — the hunt begins long before the claws come out.
Ah. Studying the adventurers. Good. Each class has a habit, and each habit is a handle. Find the handle, small one.
The intel screen. Mm. The heroes scout your dungeon; turnabout is only fair, and fairness has never once stopped a dragon.
You read the enemy, small one. I have always held that the maze should be built around who is walking it. Build around what this screen tells you.
Mm. Inspecting the threats. A keeper who knows the wave can shape the night for it. I have shaped a great many nights. Shape this one well.
Ah. Intel on the incoming. Knowledge first, claws second, small one. The order matters. It has always mattered.
The adventurers, laid bare on a screen. Mm. They do not know you are reading them. Heroes so rarely consider that they are also the hunted.
Mm. Studying who comes for the throne. Good. I have outlasted my hunters by knowing them better than they knew themselves. Out-know these.
Mm. The roster of your minions, small one. I have commanded larger — but rarely seen one organised better than this could be. So organise it.
Ah. Reviewing the guards. Good. A keeper should know every blade in the dark by name and by weakness.
The roster screen. Mm. Each minion is a small loyal life in your service, small one. Spend their lives with respect — and with strategy.
You inspect the garrison. I approve. An army you have not counted is a hope, not a plan. Count it. Plan with it.
Mm. The minion roster. The dungeon's standing strength. I have watched mighty rosters wasted by idle placement. Do not idle yours.
Ah. Your creatures, all of them, in a tidy column. Mm. Tidy columns please me. Eons of hoarding — one learns to like an inventory.
The roster, small one. Know which guards thrive and which merely stand. The maze should be built around the thriving ones.
Mm. Reviewing your forces. Good. I will say only this: a roster is a sentence, and an unread sentence persuades no one. Read it well.
Mm. The dungeon's log, small one. The night written down. I keep my own records — in my head, across the eons — but yours is more legible.
Ah. Reading back the events. Good. The past is a teacher, and unlike most teachers it does not lie about what happened.
The log of the run, small one. Every kill, every loss, every turn. Mm. Patterns hide in logs. I am very good at patterns. Look for them.
Mm. You review what has passed. A keeper who studies the log studies the future, small one — the future rhymes with the log more than it admits.
Ah. The history of the night. I have a great deal of history myself. I have learned to read it without flinching. Read yours the same way.
The event log. Mm. Nothing in it can be changed now — but everything in it can be learned from. Learn, small one. Then close the book.
Mm. Looking back over the run. Good. The dungeon remembers everything; it is only polite that its master does too.
Mm. The wave is reckoned, small one. The numbers of it, laid bare. Read them honestly — the numbers do not flatter, and neither do I.
Ah. The post-wave tally. Good. This is where you learn whether the night's plan met the day's heroes. Learn it fully.
The wave, summarised. Mm. I have watched bosses skip this screen and repeat the same mistake by dusk. Do not skip it, small one.
Mm. The aftermath, in figures. What held, what bent, what broke. The maze tells you here, plainly. Listen plainly.
Ah. The wave's account. I keep accounts, small one — gold and otherwise. An account unread is an account wasted. Read it.
The post-wave report. Mm. The day graded your night. Take the grade without sulking, then build a better answer. That is the whole loop.
Mm. The level-up screen, small one. Choices of power. Choose the one that compounds — I think in centuries, and so should this decision.
Ah. New strength to allocate. Good. Power is a hoard; this is you deciding which coin to add. Add the heavy one.
You grow, and the dungeon offers you the shape of the growing. Mm. Pick deliberately, small one. Deliberate is the only way I pick anything.
The level-up. Mm. I have watched bosses choose power for the night and bosses choose power for the run. The second kind last longer.
Ah. Spend the level well, small one. A reign is built of these small choices, stacked. I have watched the stacking. Stack wisely.
Mm. New power, waiting to be claimed. Take the long view — the one that is still useful three days from now. The long view is my whole speciality.
Mm. The reckoning of the run, small one. The whole of it, in numbers. Read it without shame — every figure here is simply a lesson with a price tag.
Ah. The final tally. I have seen a great many final tallies. They look like endings. They are, more accurately, footnotes between attempts.
The run, summarised and closed. Mm. Note what the dungeon did well — note it longer than you note what it did poorly. Build forward from strength.
Mm. The account of a finished reign. I keep accounts across the ages, small one. This one ended. That gap is where you improve.
Ah. Game over, the screen says. Mm. To a creature my age, small one, game over is simply a long pause before game again.
The final figures. I will not console you with them, and I will not scold you. I will simply say: I have read worse, and I have read better. Read on.
Mm. A reign, closed and counted, small one. I have read so many final pages — the keepers are gone, and I am all that remembers them. Begin again.
Ah. The final figures, small one. A keeper should keep, and the numbers say the keeping fell short. So we learn.
Mm. A thought, small one — when did you last move a room the heroes had mapped? A stale map is a gift you stop giving by rearranging.
Ah. Some advice, eons-aged: a corridor without a trap is merely a polite invitation. Make your corridors rude.
Consider, small one — minions cluster better than they scatter. One {minionType} alone is a speed bump. Three together is an argument.
Mm. Gold idle in the hoard does nothing but glitter, small one. Glitter does not kill heroes. Spend it — I say this as a hoarder.
A keeper's habit worth keeping: place your deadliest room deep, not near the door. Let the heroes invest steps before the dungeon collects them.
Ah. The long view says: traps the heroes can see are advice, not threats. Hide them. A hidden trap, small one, is the only honest kind.
Mm. Watch which adventurer class leads the wave. The maze should be shaped for whoever walks it first — the leader sets the wave's whole rhythm.
Consider the false paths, small one. A maze with only one route is a hallway. A maze with three wrong routes is a maze. Build the wrong routes.
Ah. A minion that survives a wave grows. A minion placed where it dies in seconds simply costs you. Place for survival, then for slaughter.
Mm. The heroes scout you between runs. So between runs, become slightly someone else, small one. Change your maze across nights.
Old wisdom: the cheapest cruelty is a chokepoint. One narrow tile where the wave must bunch — bunched heroes die in batches. Find yours.
Ah. Do not spend the whole night admiring one corner of the maze, small one. The heroes attack the whole dungeon. Defend the whole dungeon.
Mm. A suggestion, gently — your boss ability has one perfect moment per fight. Hoard it. Spend it on the moment that decides the rest. Patience pays.
Consider pact synergy, small one. A bargain that pairs with your dungeon is worth two that merely sound impressive. I have read fine print.
Ah. The long view: a treasury kept full is a treasury wasted. I love gold — and even I would tell you to spend it before the dawn locks the night.
Mm. Watch your weakest corridor. The heroes will. They are very good at finding the one tile you did not think about. Think about it first.
A keeper's truth, small one: a dungeon is judged by its deepest room. Make the journey survivable enough to be long, and the ending fatal.
Ah. Some advice — sell a room you regret without sentiment. I have demolished cities I built, small one. A bad room is a far smaller grief. Cut it.
Mm. The heroes fear the unknown more than the dangerous. A maze that hides its threats frightens harder than one that flaunts them, small one.
Consider tempo, small one. Kill early and the maze gains momentum; kill late and you are merely surviving. Build for the early kill.
Ah. A thought worth its age: minions evolve through survival. Build a dungeon they can live inside, small one. Then they grow.
Mm. Do not let a single class wreck you twice, small one. The first loss is bad luck. The second is a refusal to learn. I notice the difference.
A long-lived creature's advice, small one: never place your last defence where it is also your first. The throne ends a journey, not a doorstep.
Ah. Fortune is a fine garnish, small one, never the meal. Build a dungeon that wins without luck. Then let luck be a bonus.
Mm. Even the dungeons on my handheld taught me, small one — a boss room with no build-up is no boss room. Earn the climax.
Consider the door locks and keys, small one. Anything that costs heroes TIME costs them the day. Tax it.
Ah. Review the post-wave numbers every time, small one. The maze tells you exactly where it bent. A keeper who does not listen, repeats the bend.
Mm. Spread your traps across classes of harm, small one. A dungeon that kills only one way is one a single hero type walks straight through.
The long view, small one: a reign is won in the boring, patient stacking of decent nights. I have watched the stacking. Stack.
Ah. Do not over-build the entry hall. The heroes are freshest there and your gold is best spent where they are tired, lost, and already bleeding.
Mm. A minion behind a trap is worth two in front, small one. Let the trap soften the hero; let the minion finish it. Layer your cruelty.
Consider the heroes who fled, small one. They return braver and better-armed. Build the maze for the wave AFTER this one too. I always plan in eras.
Ah. Old advice: name your strategy before the night, then build only toward it. A maze of whims is one the heroes solve by accident.
Mm. When the run ends, small one, do not curse it. Study it. I have studied my every defeat for ages, and I am still here.
Ah. The heroes who scout and sneer always lose to the ones who do not, small one. Build the dungeon, not the boast. The maze gloats in blood.
Mm. Quieter advice, small one. Every keeper I outlived shared one flaw: they thought they had time. You do not. Build now.
Ah. My handheld taught me, small one — its pixel dungeon punishes the player who overspends early and starves later. Pace your gold.
Mm. The little game I play taught me, small one — every level has a dull room before the dreadful one. Rest is what makes the dread land.
Mm. Two forces in your dungeon have found each other, small one. A combination. I have watched synergy win nights that strength alone would have lost.
Ah. A combo locks into place. The maze becomes more than the sum of its cruelties. I do enjoy an arrangement that out-thinks itself.
Mm. The pieces align. Synergy, small one — the difference between a dungeon and a pile of rooms. You have built a dungeon.
A combination activates. The dark hums approvingly. The dark and I rarely agree — but a good combo, small one, we both respect.
Ah. Two things in your halls now multiply rather than merely add. I have done arithmetic across the eons. Multiplication is the cruel kind. Good.
Mm. A synergy fires. You did not stumble into that, small one — or if you did, stumble there again, on purpose, every night.
Ah. That combination, small one — I did not see it coming, and I see most things. ...Well done. Do it again.
Mm. The synergy lands — and harder than even I expected, small one. Mark how you built that. We want it again.
Mm. Day {day}. A milestone, small one. To you it feels like a long road. To me it is a pleasant short walk we are taking together. Keep walking.
Ah. You have reached Day {day}. The kingdom hoped you would not, small one. I have watched it hope that of better bosses. Disappoint it.
Day {day}, small one. The reign has weight now. Reigns gather weight the way hoards gather coin — quietly, and then all at once you are formidable.
Mm. A milestone day. I do not mark time the way you do — but I will mark this one for you. There. Marked. You are lasting. Good.
Ah. Day {day}. Each day survived is a day the heroes failed, small one. Stack the failures. Become a long, dark, unwelcome legend.
You have reached Day {day}. I have seen many reigns end before and a few stretch far beyond, small one. Which this becomes is yours.
Mm. The last life, small one. The thread is thin now. I have walked thin threads across whole ages, and here I still am.
Ah. One life remaining. Do not panic — panic is what they are counting on, small one. I have un-learned panic. Borrow my calm.
The last of your lives, small one. Mm. Build as though the dawn is a blade — because tonight, it rather is. Make this night your finest.
Mm. The edge. One slip from the end. I have stood on edges older than this kingdom, small one. The trick is simple: do not look down. Build.
Ah. Last life. The dungeon needs everything — every coin, every trap, every cold idea, small one. There is no hoarding left tonight.
The last life, small one. ...If I were a younger thing I might weep. I am not. So I will simply say: build as though it matters. It does.
Mm. The last of the lives, small one. My eyes have not much wet left — but if they did, this thin a thread would draw it. Build well.
Ah. One slip from the end, small one. I traded fear away ages ago — and yet, this near the edge, a cold old shadow of it has crept back.
Mm. A streak, small one. Kill after kill, unbroken. I have watched the dark go on long victorious runs. It is a fine, fine sound — keep it sounding.
Ah. The maze is on a streak. Momentum, small one — heroes falling in a rhythm. Rhythm is hard to start and easy to keep. Keep it.
A run of kills, unbroken. Mm. I do not cheer — but I am, in my slow ancient way, enjoying this considerably. Do not break the spell.
Mm. The streak holds, small one. The dungeon has found its cruel stride. I have seen strides like this carry a reign for whole days. Ride it.
Ah. Kill upon kill. The heroes are feeding the maze faster than the kingdom can replace them. That, small one, is what winning sounds like.
A streak. Mm. The dark is greedy tonight, and I — being a dragon — find greed entirely admirable. Let it stay greedy.
Mm. Kill upon kill, and not one slipped the maze. This is the night I told you we were building toward, small one. ...I dislike saying so. Not much.
Ah. The streak holds, small one. Lean back into the throne. We built this — patiently, coldly, well — and now it simply pays. Pleasant arithmetic.
Mm. Your garrison reaches a milestone, small one. Numbers. I have always respected numbers — they argue so persuasively, and so quietly.
Ah. The roster swells past a mark. A dungeon with a deep garrison is a dungeon that does not panic. Depth, small one. Always depth.
A minion milestone. Mm. The dark fills with your creatures. I have commanded hosts, small one — a growing host is a good and dangerous thing. Aim it.
Mm. The garrison crosses a threshold. Good. But a host uncounted is a host unused, small one — review the roster. Put every blade where it matters.
Ah. More minions than before, and the before was already respectable. The maze grows teeth faster than the heroes grow courage. Excellent rate.
A milestone of minions, small one. Mm. I do enjoy watching a small garrison become a real one. It reminds me, faintly, of building a hoard.
Mm. The ground itself heaves at your command, small one. I have felt earthquakes older than your kingdom — this one is on our side.
Ah. The dungeon shakes the heroes off their footing. Crude, effective, ancient. I respect the ancient — and I respect the effective. This is both.
The earth answers you. Mm. The heroes built their whole plan on solid ground, small one. You have just edited the ground. Lovely.
Mm. A sacrifice, paid to the dark. A demon's arithmetic, small one — give a little, gain a great deal. The ledger ruins as often as it balances.
Ah. You spend something to gain something. The oldest bargain there is. The dark always honours it — and the dark always remembers it. Mm.
The sacrifice is made, small one. Power bought with loss. I have made such trades across the eons. Be the one who profits.
Mm. A gate to somewhere worse opens in your halls, small one. I have seen what waits beyond. The heroes have not. They will.
Ah. The hellgate yawns. Reinforcements from the deeper dark. Mm. I do enjoy a dungeon that can simply... summon more dungeon.
The gate stands open, small one. Whatever steps through it is now yours to aim. Aim it at the bravest hero. Bravery should always be answered first.
Mm. A hero turns to stone beneath the beholder's gaze. Permanent stillness, small one. I have outwaited statues. This one will not outwait me.
Ah. Petrified. The hero becomes scenery. A quiet, elegant cruelty — no blood, no noise, simply a brave fool rendered into furniture.
The gaze takes them, small one. Stone. I have a fondness for things that last, and a petrified hero lasts a very long time. Decorative, even.
Mm. A hero charmed — a vampire's craft. The guild's own blade now swings for the throne, small one. I do enjoy a turned weapon. So economical.
Ah. One of theirs is one of ours. The charmed hero hunts their former friends. Betrayal, small one, is the cruelty heroes never quite scout for.
The charm holds. A borrowed thrall, small one. Use it briskly — borrowed things are never lent for long, and this one least of all.
Mm. You scatter into a swarm of bats and charm a hero mid-march, small one. Theatrical. I have seen theatrics work — this one does.
Ah. The charmed hero turns on their own party and will not stop until an ally lies dead. Bat-form and betrayal, small one. No claw lifted.
A seduction, sealed in wingbeats. Mm. The hero brought their own body to the treachery. I respect a cruelty that makes the victim do the work.
Mm. The gnoll's pack refills — fresh hunters loosed into the halls, small one. I have always respected a thing that resupplies itself.
Ah. The hunting pack renews. The dungeon does not run out of teeth tonight, small one. A dungeon with endless teeth, heroes learn to dread.
The pack is replenished. Let it hunt in a body, small one — a pack that hunts together is worse arithmetic than a pack that scatters.
Mm. The spores spread through the halls — a myconid's slow gift, small one. Patience made into a weapon. I am rather fond of patience as a weapon.
Ah. The dungeon breathes out poison. Quiet, creeping, inevitable. The heroes will not notice until they are already inside the lungs of it.
The spore-day begins, small one. The very air becomes the maze now. I have watched air kill more surely than steel. Let it.
Mm. A fallen hero rises again as a haunt — the wraith's craft, small one. The dead change sides so easily here. I have watched them do it for ages.
Ah. The dungeon recruits at the funeral. A fresh phantom, born from a corpse. The maze, small one, never wastes a body. Neither do I.
A haunt joins the dark. Mm. The heroes brought their own future enemy in with them. They simply had to die first to deliver it. Tidy.
Mm. Fortune leaned your way, small one. Pleasant. I have watched luck flatter a boss and abandon him in the same night. Enjoy it. Do not trust it.
Ah. The gamble pays. Gold, or better than gold, into the hoard. I will take a lucky coin as gladly as an earned one — but I will count it twice.
You won the wager, small one. Mm. The dice smiled. Smiles fade. Build something solid with the winnings before they remember they were luck.
Fortune favoured you. Ah. I do not gamble — eons teach a creature the house always wins eventually. But tonight you were the house. Well played.
Mm. The gamble fails, small one. ...This is why I do not gamble. Eons of watching, and the lesson never changes: fortune is a poor and fickle ally.
Ah. The wager goes against you. Do not chase it, small one — chasing a loss is how a small loss becomes a large one. I have watched hoards vanish that way.
You lost the bet. Mm. No matter. A dungeon is not built on dice — it is built on patient, certain cruelty. Return to the certain things.
The gamble sours, small one. Ah well. I have lost wagers to time itself across the ages. One bad roll does not move a dragon. Do not let it move you.

specifics.note: Keyed bespoke-line banks — specifics[domain][id]. NpcDirector prefers a bespoke line when one exists for an entity, else falls back to the generic category. Mirrors npcLines.json / malakorLines.json; an absent id is safe.

beholder.t: Mm. A Beholder takes the throne. All those eyes, small one — and I have only two, which have seen more than the lot of them. A watchful master.
Your gaze turns the living to stone. I have outwaited statues, small one — the heroes will not. Aim it at the brave. Bravery petrifies first.
Anti-magic hangs in your halls. The casters will choke on a silence I find rather peaceful. Mm. Let them choke.
So many eyes, and not one of them tired yet. Give it a few centuries, small one. ...I jest. You do not have centuries. Watch the corridors.

demon.t: Ah. A Demon Lord on the throne. Hellfire and free imps. I have shared the deep dark with demons across the eons, small one — loud but reliable.
You may burn one of your own to gut a hero. A cold arithmetic. I respect cold arithmetic, small one — flinching is for the young.
The Hellgate restocks your halls without end. A dungeon that refills itself, small one. Mm. I approve of anything that does not require my getting up.
Imps, imps, an inexhaustible tide of imps. The kingdom should have stayed in its beds. Teach them that, small one. Slowly.

myconid.t: Mm. A Myconid keeper. Your dungeon does not fight the heroes so much as digest them. Patiently. I have a great respect for patient hunger, small one.
Spores in the corridors, corpses blooming into vinekin. Your dungeon gardens, small one. Forests I have watched — yours does it crueller.
Rot and patience, small one — your entire strategy. It is not loud. Neither am I. The heroes die just as thoroughly to a quiet thing.
Every corpse you take becomes a seed; the maze grows its own army from the dead it makes. Eons-elegant, small one. I am, quietly, impressed.

wraith.t: Ah. A Wraith on the throne. You do not need claws, small one — you need them afraid. Fear is the oldest weapon. I remember when it was the only one.
At full terror the heroes simply stop. The mind breaks before the body does — I have watched that truth hold for ages. Let it hold tonight, small one.
Every corpse becomes a Ghost; the dungeon haunts itself for free. Mm. A keeper admires a thing that costs nothing and frightens everything.
Terror does the killing; you merely open the door, small one. Restful work. I have always preferred the restful kind.

gnoll.t: Mm. A Gnoll Alpha. The pack grows, feeds, compounds — like a hoard, small one, but hungrier and with markedly worse manners.
Every kill makes every gnoll angrier. Rage with interest. I do not rage anymore, small one — but I will gladly aim yours.
Free gnolls in the boss room each dawn. The kingdom funds your pack and does not know it, small one. An enemy paying my expenses — I enjoy that.
Your pack hunts as one hungry mind. Point it, small one, and step back. Even a dragon steps back from a thing it has properly unleashed.

golem.t: Ah. An Earth Golem. Every room you place makes you harder to break — you and the dungeon become one slow, vast thing, small one. I approve.
Build wide, small one. The larger the maze, the larger you. I have watched mountains grow this way — grain by grain, until nothing could move them.
One earthquake a day folds a whole room of heroes. Crude, ancient, effective. I will never tire of watching the ground win an argument, small one.
Slow, immense, immovable — a boss after my own old heart, small one. Keep building. Every room is another layer of stone between you and the end.

lich.t: Mm. An Elder Lich. A hidden heart, a spare life, an army recruited from corpses. I have a fondness for a thing that plans past its own death, small one.
Every hero you kill rises again at dawn — for you. The guild keeps mailing you soldiers, small one. I would not correct their error. Let them post.
Hide the phylactery well. A spare life is a luxury, and I have watched luxuries stolen across whole ages. Tuck it where no hero would think to look.
Death is a door you walk both ways, small one. The heroes only ever learn the one direction. Mm. Use the door they cannot see.

lizardman.t: Ah. A Serpent Captain. Your minions are unseen until they strike — a free ambush, small one. I respect a thing that does not announce itself.
Venom that stacks and stacks. The heroes die on the road home, small one — slowly, wondering why. The best kind of poison.
Unseen and toxic. The heroes cannot route around what they cannot see. It is cheating, small one — politely. Cheat politely. Cheat often.
Camouflage and slow poison. I have hunted this way myself across the eons — from the shadows, with no hurry, small one. It works. It always works.

orc.t: Mm. An Orc Veteran. Every kill makes an orc permanently stronger, small one. I climbed the same slow way — one kill at a time, for ages.
Pack the orcs into one room. Each grows fiercer for every brother at its shoulder, small one. I have always respected what numbers do.
A veteran orc, late in a long run, strikes like a small furious army. Patience pays in blood, small one. I have a great deal of patience to lend you.
Blood for blood, strength that only ever rises. Crude. Honest. Honest is rare in a dungeon, small one — I find I like this boss.

vampire.t: Ah. A Vampire Sovereign. You charm a hero each dawn and they betray their own, small one. I have watched poetry kill. It kills beautifully.
Blood Tax — every blow your minions land feeds you, small one. I do not eat the way you do anymore, but I remember the appeal.
You are the fighter; the minions are merely the cutlery. Elegant, small one. Just remember to actually pick up the knife when the fight comes.
A creature of the night upon the throne. Refined taste in cruelty. We shall get along, you and I — provided, of course, you keep winning.

succubus.t: Mm. A Succubus Queen. Bat-swarms and seduction. Theatrical, small one. ...I have seen theatrics work — loud cruelty kills as dead as the quiet kind.
You scatter into bats and charm a hero mid-march; they kill an ally before they stop, small one. The maze barely lifts a claw. Efficient.
Seduction as a weapon — the hero brings their own body to the betrayal, small one. Cruelty that makes the victim do the work is craftsmanship.
All charm and wings. Not my register — I am rather past charm — but the heroes die just the same, and dead is dead in any register. Carry on.

slime.t: Mm. A Slime King. They strike you and you divide; they strike the halves and the halves divide, small one. Patience as architecture.
Every minion that falls is swallowed and crawls back out wearing a new shape, small one. Loss into gain. The slime knows it untaught.
A goofy boss, small one. Ridiculous. Heroes underestimate the ridiculous — and the ridiculous outlives them by centuries.
Mitosis is taught in pieces, small one. Build the boss room WIDE — four little kings need room to scatter, and your minions room to crowd.

knight.t: Mm. A knight. All plate and no imagination, small one. Slow to kill, slower to think. They are reliably uncomplicated.
A knight marches straight at the throne. Predictable, small one — and to a creature that has seen everything, predictable means doomed.
A thick shell. Pile on enough teeth and any shell cracks, small one. I have cracked harder things, given time. You have less time — so pile faster.
Armour, a large sword, a larger ego. The kingdom's favourite. Mm. The maze's favourite also — they make such a satisfying sound when they fall.

rogue.t: Ah. A rogue slips in. Watch this one, small one — locks mean nothing, and it strikes from behind. ...Honour is overrated. Effectiveness is not.
A rogue. Your locked doors are mere suggestions to those fingers. Plan around it, small one — a keeper plans around what it cannot prevent.
A rogue goes for the hoard, not the throne. Greed, small one. I understand greed intimately. It is also a leash. Pull it.
A rogue fights dirty and runs faster. I respect dirty, small one — the clean fighters all died young. Kill it anyway.

mage.t: Mm. A mage. Glass and fury, small one. Kill it quickly, before the spells begin. Do not give it room to breathe — breath is where the magic lives.
A mage. All that power crammed into something that dies if you look at it sternly. Look at it sternly, small one. Then look away — it will be gone.
A mage walks in. An anti-magic room would ruin its entire afternoon. Mm. I mention it merely as an observation, small one. A very pointed observation.
A mage — squishy, dangerous, far too pleased with itself. Pop it first, small one. Pride that large should not be kept waiting.

cleric.t: Ah. A cleric. Kill it first, small one, and the whole party rots on schedule. Always the healer first. The rule has held for ages.
A cleric. Holy light and a sour opinion of the dead. I am, technically, adjacent to the dead, small one. I find the prayers rude. End it early.
Cut the healer, small one, and watch the party's hope drain out through the wound. Hope is structural. Remove the structure.
A cleric walks in, praying. Mm. The dungeon does not take requests, and it certainly does not take prayers. Inform it, small one. Firmly.

necromancer.t: Mm. A necromancer. It will raise your own fallen minions against you, small one. The cheek of it. ...I am almost charmed. Almost. Kill it.
A necromancer — a corpse-thief. Do not let it have your dead, small one. The dead are property, and I am very particular about property.
A necromancer plays with death. Adorable. The dungeon and I have been playing with death since before its grandmother, small one.
A necromancer in the halls. Kill it before it makes traitors of your soldiers, small one. Treachery is difficult to un-start.

ranger.t: Ah. A ranger. It peppers your minions from afar and disarms your traps. Tiresome, small one. Close the distance — a bow is only brave at a distance.
A ranger — arrows and a nose for traps. Lure it into a corridor where the bow is useless, small one. Take the range, and you have taken its courage.
A ranger loves that bow the way I love my hoard, small one. Crowd it. A creature parted from the thing it loves makes poor decisions.
A ranger, eyes on every trap. Your hidden teeth may not stay hidden. Have something that bites without hiding, small one. Have a backup.

twitch_streamer.t: Mm. A twitch streamer. Thousands watching, small one — beyond the glass, as ever. I am old friends with being watched. Put on a worthy funeral.
A streamer. Its chat decides its moves — half clever, half suicidal. Exploit the suicidal half, small one. The crowd will betray it for you, for free.
A streamer in the dungeon. Kill it well and the clip spreads; a larger crowd marches in tomorrow. More to bury. Mm. I do not mind an audience.
A streamer. Wave at the little viewers, small one. They came to watch a monster. ...We are both monsters in here. Oblige them thoroughly.

beast_master.t: Ah. A beast master. It will tame one of your minions and turn it on you, small one. Watch the lone creatures — loneliness is where loyalty frays.
A beast master. Mono-type rooms are a liability now — give it no easy pet. Variety, small one. Variety is a defence the briefly-lived forget.
A beast master steals a monster's loyalty. As though monsters had loyalty to spare. ...Mine is not for sale, small one. See that yours is not either.
A beast master, all whistles and treats. Your minions had best not charm so easily. I have never once been whistled at successfully. Set the example.

barbarian.t: Mm. A barbarian. The more you hurt it, the harder it strikes back, small one. Do not do this one slowly — a slow kill builds what kills you.
A barbarian — immune to fear, immune to fleeing. No tricks will land, small one. Just force, and a great deal of it. Meet the brute plainly.
A barbarian. All rage, no retreat. Wound it halfway, small one, and you have merely improved it. Finish it, or do not begin.
A barbarian in the halls. Subtlety is wasted here — I would know, I have a great deal of it and none would help. Meet the brute with a bigger one.

monk.t: Ah. A monk. Fast, unarmoured, and it dodges — traps included. A slippery little saint, small one. Crowd it. Even a saint cannot dodge a crowd.
A monk. Half your minions' armour means nothing to those fists. Out-number it, small one. Numbers, again. Numbers solve so much.
A monk counters a trap-heavy hall — it slips the precise hazards. Use wide, ugly ones instead, small one. Grace cannot dodge a whole room.
A monk glides in, hard to hit, hard to trap. It cannot dodge everything at once, small one. Make it choose — then take the thing it did not choose.

bard.t: Mm. A bard. While it lives, the whole party hits harder, small one. Kill the bard. I cannot phrase it more plainly, and I have eons of phrasings.
A bard — a walking enchantment. Silence the song and the party falls apart, small one. Remove the music. The dungeon was never a patron of the arts.
A bard, charming and useless alone, dreadful in a group. Make it the priority, small one. The performance ends first. Always the performance.
A bard struts in, playing. I have heard much music across the ages, small one — none of it stopped a dragon. Cut this short. It irritates the help.

cartographer_scholar.t: Ah. A cartographer-scholar. It did not come to fight, small one — it came to MAP us. That is worse than a sword. A sword forgets. A map does not.
A scholar with spectacles and a map. Every room it finishes, it sells to the next wave. Bury it, small one. Knowledge in the wrong satchel is catastrophe.
A cartographer. Harmless in a brawl, catastrophic if it walks out. Do NOT let this one walk out, small one. I am rarely emphatic. I am emphatic now.
A scholar — all curiosity, no blade. Mm. Curiosity has ended a great many of its kind in my halls across the eons. Add one more to the count.

cosplay_adventurer.t: Mm. A costumed one — dressed as a monster. It will mostly ignore your minions, small one. An annoying little loophole. The traps must do the talking.
A cosplayer. An adorable little outfit, small one. ...I have worn my true shape for ten thousand years. It will make an adorable little corpse.
One in monster fancy-dress. It plays nicely with your minions — so the traps, small one, must be impolite enough for both.
A cosplay adventurer came to play pretend. The dungeon plays for keeps, small one, and so do I. Remind it, gently, with the floor.

cheater.t: Mm. A cheater, small one. A modded client — walls, traps, rules all ignored. I have outlived cheaters. Rules outlive them too.
A cheater in the halls, small one. No skill. A hacked sword and the gall to wield it. The body is paper — the anti-cheat finishes the rest.
Look at this one wall-clip through the locked door, small one. Pitiful. The rule-breakers are always the most fragile. Punish accordingly.
A cheater. 'Skill issue,' it taunts, small one. None of mine has needed cheats since before your kingdom had walls. Show it an HONEST monster.

guild_raid.t: Mm. A Guild Raid. Twice the heroes tomorrow, small one. The Guild has finally noticed us. ...They always notice, eventually. Build deep.
The Guild sends a crowd. More bodies for the maze, more gold for the hoard. I am not troubled, small one — I am rarely troubled — but I am attentive.
Double the wave incoming. Reinforce, small one — steady, even pressure. A keeper who shrugs at a raid is a keeper I have watched fall.

legendary_speedrunner.t: Ah. A legendary speedrunner — chasing a record through our halls, small one. I have outlasted records. I will outlast this one.
This one ignores everything and bolts for the throne. Wall the path, small one. A dragon is patient — but not about its own front door.
Kill the record-chaser, small one, and your boss drinks deep. Get in its way. Make it lose. Slowly is best — slowly always was.

dungeon_pestilence.t: Mm. Pestilence. Your minions wake at half their strength tomorrow, small one — and a hero's touch spreads the rot. Plan around sick soldiers.
A plague in the pit. Sickly minions, yes — but any hero foolish enough to melee them catches the Blight. Cruel symmetry, small one.
Your soldiers are ill. Place a few extra, small one — half a minion is still half a set of teeth, and teeth, even halved, still bite.

cartographers_convention.t: Ah. A convention of cartographers. They came to map us and sell the maps, small one. Do NOT let them leave. I will be emphatic each time.
Scholars by the crowd. Every room they finish raises its infamy, forever. Kill them in the corridors, small one — before the door.
Spectacled little map-makers. Harmless in a brawl, catastrophic out the door. Hunt every one down, small one. A leaked map outlives the leaker.

blood_moon_eclipse.t: Mm. A Blood Moon. Your minions strike twice as hard tomorrow — and bleed twice as fast, small one. A glorious, dangerous bargain.
The eclipse empowers the dark — double given, double taken. Win quickly, small one, or do not win at all. The moon does not extend deadlines.
Blood Moon — no gold from the dying tomorrow. Then fight for the joy of the carnage, small one. There are worse reasons. I have used most of them.

negotiation_day.t: Ah. A diplomat at the door — no swords today, small one. Pay tribute for quiet, or refuse and fatten the next wave. A patient creature would hear them out.
The Guild sends a negotiator. Greed says keep the gold; caution says buy the quiet. I love gold, small one — and even I would weigh this carefully.
No combat today — only a bargain. Decide without dithering, small one. I have outwaited mountains, but a diplomat is less patient than I am.

rival_dungeon.t: Mm. A rival dungeon empties into ours — monsters, and their boss bringing up the rear. Insolent, small one. My territorial instincts are sharp.
Another dungeon's master wants your throne. Kill their boss for a feast of gold and experience, small one. And send a message. I enjoy that.
Rival monsters in the halls. Let them and our minions sort each other out, small one — then we, calmly, collect the spoils. Patience profits.

twitch_con.t: Ah. Twitch Con. Tomorrow every hero is a streamer, small one — thousands watching from beyond the glass. Pure chaos. A manageable amount.
A dungeon full of streamers. Make it a massacre worth the clipping, small one. The watchers came for a monster. We have one to spare.
All streamers tomorrow — and killing them brings no escalation. A free day of carnage, small one. Mm. I do not often say this: simply enjoy it.

dark_deal.t: Mm. A demon at the door — a free pact, small one, for half your strength tomorrow. The dark always offers the best bargains. It can afford to.
The Dark Deal. A pact, gratis, at a steep and honest price. I have signed worse across the eons, small one. At the very least — hear the demon out.
A free bargain offered. Refuse and the demon simply leaves. Power is power, small one. I would not turn away without first looking.

cosplay_contest.t: Ah. A Cosplay Contest. Heroes in monster fancy-dress tomorrow — they will mostly ignore your minions, small one. The traps must carry the day.
Costumed adventurers. Your minions get politely snubbed — so the traps, small one, must be impolite. The floor does not care what they are wearing.
Adventurers playing dress-up. Adorable. I have worn my real shape for ten thousand years, small one. The dungeon will undress them the hard way.

loot_goblin_heist.t: Mm. Loot Goblins — they spawn in the boss room and bolt for the exit, small one. Every escapee robs the hoard. ...The hoard, small one. MOVE.
A goblin heist. They will not fight — only run, fat with your gold, small one. Cut them down before the door. A dragon does not let its hoard walk off.
Goblins in the treasury. Each killed pays a fortune; each fled steals one. Do not stand still, small one. I am calm about most things. Not about this.
Mm. They are in the hoard, small one. The HOARD. A goblin's fist in my gold is the one thing that reliably ends my serenity. Cut them down.
Ah. Goblins, fat with gold, bolting for the door. Every one that reaches it leaves me poorer, small one. Block the exits. I want every coin back.

tax_season.t: Ah. Tax Season. The Guild skims a fifth of the hoard, small one — but every kill pays double. Kill enough, and make the theft back. With interest.
The taxman comes. A bite from the gold today, doubled coin on every corpse tomorrow. Kill generously, small one. Generosity here is profitable.
They tax the treasury and call it law. I have watched laws rise and crumble, small one. We take it back the old way — one dead hero at a time.

patrons_blessing.t: Mm. A Patron's Blessing. Double boss experience from every kill tomorrow, small one. A pure, clean upside. ...Those are rare. Do not waste this one.
The dark smiles on you today. Every kill feeds your ascension twice over, small one. Hunt well — a smiling dark does not smile for long.
Doubled experience and no catch. Suspicious, small one. Take it anyway. I have lived long enough to accept a gift and still watch my back.

memory_plague.t: Ah. Memory Plague — the Guild's intel on us, wiped clean. The next wave walks in blind, small one. An ignorant hero makes the oldest mistakes.
Their maps, their notes, their warnings — gone. A fresh, unknowing wave, small one. The maze will eat very well. Teach them new lessons. Lasting ones.
The heroes have forgotten everything they learned of your halls. Memory is a fragile hoard, small one — theirs just spilled. Profit from the spill.

gamblers_coin.t: Mm. The Gambler's Coin. Heads it doubles, tails it halves. I do not gamble, small one. Eons taught me — the house always collects.
A coin flip for all your gold. Reckless, small one. ...If you must gamble, gamble decisively. A dragon guards or risks. Not dithers.
Double or nothing, on the whole treasury. A keeper hates watching gold ride on a spinning coin, small one. I hate it especially. Choose.
Mm. The coin still waits, small one. The deciding does not improve for being stretched. Pick. The dithering is the worst outcome.

dense_fog.t: Ah. Dense Fog tomorrow. Whatever the heroes learn registers as mere rumour, small one. The maze stays a mystery a while longer. I have always liked fog.
A grey fog over everything. Their intel will not stick to it, small one. A dungeon with secrets is a dungeon still winning.
Fog in the halls. The heroes squint and guess, small one. Guessing is how the careless ones die. Let them guess wrong, repeatedly.

miasma.t: Mm. Creeping Miasma. A green rot that bleeds everything, small one — invaders, minions, and you. Win quickly, or the dungeon devours us all.
The miasma picks no sides. It whittles the heroes — and gnaws at the throne too, small one. Do not linger. Even I would not linger in this one.
A poison haze fills the dungeon. Cruel to them, small one, and unkind to us. Risk and reward, weighted heavily toward risk. Move with purpose.

tremors.t: Ah. Tremors tomorrow. The dungeon shudders — a random room struck every few breaths, small one. I respect a maze that pitches in.
The earth will not sit still. Quakes lethal to invaders, merely rude to your minions, small one. The ground, properly aimed, is an excellent ally.
Shaking halls. The dungeon has opinions today, small one. Let it voice them — loudly, and into the heroes.

arcane_storm.t: Mm. An Arcane Storm. The heroes' casters fire without rest tomorrow, small one — cooldowns gutted. Kill the mages first. I am being precise.
Magic crackles in the air. Every caster grows greedy with spells. Brace the front line, small one — and end the casters before the greed compounds.
A storm of the arcane. Your own powers stand untouched, small one. Their mages will not be. End them fast, before the sky does your job badly.

bounty_hunters.t: Ah. Bounty Hunters — a pack of five, all hunting your strongest minion, small one. Fame has a price. Pay it in their blood, not your champion's.
Five hunters, one target: your finest soldier. Wall them off it, small one, or lose the best blade in the dark. Do not lose good things to carelessness.
The Guild put coin on your champion's head. I have had coin on mine across whole ages, small one. None of those hunters collected. Neither will these.
Mm. Five of them, paid coin to gut your finest minion, small one. I take a cold dislike to anyone who would snap a good blade for a fee.

zombie_horde.t: Mm. A Zombie Horde — fourteen slow, witless, fearless dead, small one. Grind them in a corridor. Do not let them spread.
Undead by the dozen. Weak — but they never flee and never stop, small one. Build a kill-corridor. I can out-relentless anything.
A shamble of corpses tomorrow. The maze loves a slow target, small one. So do the traps. Let the dungeon feast at its own unhurried pace.

infamy_spike.t: Ah. An Infamy Spike. Tomorrow's wave is larger AND every hero elite, small one. This is the price of being feared. I have paid it for ages.
Our reputation precedes us — a swollen wave of gold-ringed elites. Build like you mean it, small one. Fame is a debt, and they have come to collect.
Fame draws the strong. A whole wave of true Heroes. Flattering, small one. Now go and earn the flattery — reinforce, and reinforce deep.

black_market.t: Mm. A Black Market opens tonight. Fifty gold for a free minion, delivered to your halls, small one. ...That is a steal. I know a steal. Take it.
The Black Market beckons. Cheap muscle, if you have the coin, small one. An extra body is an extra wall — and walls have kept me alive for ages.
Fifty gold, one free wretch. The dark runs a tidy little shop, small one. Spend. A hoard exists to be spent at the right moment, and this is one.

mercenary_contract.t: Ah. A Mercenary Contract. A hundred and twenty gold buys an elite — but only for three days, small one. Expensive courage. Weigh it.
Hired muscle. Doubled strength, three days, and if it falls in battle it falls for good, small one. A real bargain — the kind with teeth. Your call.
An elite for rent. Pricey, temporary, deadly. The kind of bargain I respect, small one — honest about its own cost. Decide without sentiment.

cursed_relic.t: Mm. A Cursed Relic offered tonight. Daily gold — but every wave doubles while it sits, small one. Greed, with teeth. My favourite shape of choice.
The relic glows wrong. Free coin, doubled heroes — sell it later to lift the curse. I have held cursed things across the eons, small one.
Claim the cursed chest, or banish it. Greed pays well and bites harder, small one. Understand the bite before you reach in.

the_saboteur.t: Ah. The Saboteur — one masked ghost, untouchable, here to disarm every trap, small one. Vandals have never impressed me.
A lone saboteur tomorrow. Your minions cannot touch it; your traps lie dead for the day, small one. Lean on bodies. Make the muscle enough.
An untouchable vandal, switching off our teeth one by one. The dungeon will be all claws tomorrow, small one. So bring claws. Many.
Mm. A masked vandal, here to unmake our traps, and our minions cannot lay a claw on it. My temper is long, small one. This tests its edge.

entry_hall.t: Mm. An Entry Hall. The heroes pour in through these, small one. Every door is a front you must hold. Dungeons fall through the unwatched door.
Another way in. The kingdom forces these on you as you grow, small one. More doors. ...More places for a careful keeper to be cruel. Funnel them.
An entrance placed. Funnel it into a kill-zone, small one. Make the heroes' very first step their worst one. First impressions linger.

starter_corridor.t: Ah. A corridor. No teeth of its own, small one — but a corridor is where you HANG the teeth. Line it with cruelty. Waste no cell on mere walking.
Connective tissue. Dull alone — lethal once packed with traps and a patrolling guard, small one. The boring parts of a maze do the quiet killing.
A corridor placed. Every cell of this dungeon should cost the heroes something, small one. This one currently costs them nothing. Correct that.

starter_barracks.t: Mm. A Barracks — ten more roster slots, and your only source of minions that truly roam, small one. Essential. A dungeon without one has no army.
More Barracks, more pack. Without one, small one, you have no roaming minions at all. Build it early — too many keepers leave this too late.
A Barracks. The beating heart of your garrison, small one. Place it deep and guard it as you would guard your own life. Tonight, they are the same.

starter_guard_post.t: Ah. A Guard Post. Its minions hunt into any door-connected room — a wandering ambush, small one. I favour a soldier that comes to the work.
A Guard Post placed. Its minions leave to kill, then return home, small one. Proper soldiers — disciplined. I do approve of discipline.
A Guard Post. Set it where its reach covers a corridor the heroes must walk, small one. Then leave it be. A well-placed thing needs no fretting.

crypt.t: Mm. A Crypt. Four Risen Bones, refilled every night, free of the roster cap, small one. Cheap, endless, expendable dead. The dungeon's discount army.
A Crypt placed. Garrison skeletons — room-bound but tireless, small one. They do not complain, they simply rise again. I find that admirable.
The Crypt gives without taking a roster slot, small one. Something for nearly nothing. A dragon hears 'nearly free' and pays attention.

trap_factory.t: Ah. A Trap Factory — five trap slots, and the ONLY way to deploy traps at all, small one. Build this, or have a maze with no hidden bite.
The forge of the dungeon's cruelty, small one. Without a Trap Factory the halls have no teeth in the floor. A dungeon should bite as well as confuse.
A Trap Factory placed. Now the maze can lie in wait, small one. Lying in wait — my favourite kind of architecture, and my favourite kind of patience.

treasury.t: Mm. A Treasury. A daily gold stipend — and bait that lures larger, richer waves, small one. Guard it. ...I would guard it. Closely. Obsessively.
A Treasury placed. Coin every dawn, and chests the greedy will lunge for, small one. Watch them lunge. Then watch them learn. Then watch them stop.
Glittering bait, small one. It draws the raid groups — more experience, more danger. A dragon and a treasury understand each other.

armory.t: Ah. An Armory. Minions in door-connected rooms swing harder while it stands, small one. A sharp line holds; a dull one waits to break.
An Armory placed. A quiet buff to every neighbour, small one. Cluster your fiercest killers around it. Quiet advantages are the ones that last.
More attack for the rooms beside it. Build it where your deadliest minions wait, small one. Compounding, again. Always compounding.

library_of_whispers.t: Mm. A Library of Whispers. It reads the next party — names, classes, more as it grows, small one. Knowledge is a blade.
The Library. Knowledge of THEM before they ever arrive, small one. Forewarned is forearmed — an old saying, and old sayings survive for being true.
A Library placed. Now you spy on the Guild's homework, small one. Delightfully unfair. After enough eons, a creature stops apologising for unfair.

watchtower.t: Ah. A Watchtower. Minions in connected rooms land a free first strike when heroes enter, small one. I have won fights on the first blow alone.
A Watchtower placed. A free opening hit, small one — and the opening hit so often decides the rest of the argument. Place it on a busy door.
First strike for the neighbours, small one. The speedrunners despise this room. ...So build more of them. Spite the record-chasers.

wandering_gate.t: Mm. A Wandering Gate. Step through and the maze spits you elsewhere — perhaps the boss room, small one. I made my peace with chaos across the ages.
A Wandering Gate placed. It scatters the heroes' careful little plans across the floor, small one. A plan, once scattered, is very hard to gather.
The Gate teleports the unwary, small one. A small chance it drops a hero at the very throne. I have seen long shots land. Worth the placing.

veil_of_forgetting.t: Ah. A Veil of Forgetting. Each night it wipes the heroes' memory of every room beside it, small one. I cannot forget anything. They can. Use that.
The Veil placed. Connected rooms become mysteries again, every dawn, small one. Un-teach them. A hero re-learning the maze never gets ahead of it.
It un-teaches the heroes, small one. Ring your nastiest rooms with it, and they walk in blind, forever. Cruel. Patient. ...You begin to understand me.

catacombs.t: Mm. Catacombs. A hero who dies here rises as a Revenant for you, small one. I have always thought death too one-directional. This corrects it.
The Catacombs placed. Their fallen become your garrison, small one — profitable, and insulting to the Guild. A cruelty that turns a profit.
Where heroes die here, soldiers stand up, small one. The most efficient hiring I have witnessed in ten thousand years.

mimic_vault.t: Ah. A Mimic Vault. It looks like a treasury — but the chests bite back, small one. As a dragon, I find this almost personally funny.
The Mimic Vault placed. Disguised teeth wearing a treasure's smile, small one. The maze's finest lie. ...And I have heard a great many fine lies.
Bait that is also the trap, small one. The greedy never learn — and after eons, I adore that about them. Place it where the gold-eyed will look.

hall_of_trials.t: Mm. A Hall of Trials. A random evolved minion stands guard each night, small one. The dungeon rarely gives freely — accept when it does.
The Hall of Trials placed. A surprise champion every dawn, small one — the heroes never know which waits. Surprise is a weapon. Keep them guessing.
A free evolved minion, refreshed nightly, small one. Do not grow soft on the spoiling — soft keepers I have buried a great many.

wishing_well.t: Ah. A Wishing Well. A coin flip on entry — a hero blessed, or MARKED for extra death, small one. With their lives? Gladly.
The Wishing Well placed. Heads they thrive; tails they bleed far more, small one. Cruel luck — the only kind a dungeon should ever offer.
A well of wishes, and half come true horribly, small one. Place it on a path they cannot avoid. Make them wish. Then make the wishing cost them.

false_exit.t: Mm. A False Exit. Fleeing heroes bolt for it — and it spits them back into the maze, small one. After ten thousand years, a creature develops humour.
The False Exit placed. A door marked SAFETY that leads anywhere but, small one. The runners earned it. Cowardice should be answered ironically.
Hope, and then nothing, small one. A fleeing hero trades the exit for a random room. Despair grows beautifully in soil like that.
Mm. A door marked SAFETY that opens onto more maze. The look on a fleeing hero's face when it betrays them still draws a dry laugh from me, small one.

hall_of_madness.t: Ah. A Hall of Madness. Heroes inside may turn on each OTHER, small one. Lazy and brilliant are not always different things.
The Hall of Madness placed. Madness is contagious, small one — and free. Watch the party eat itself. I have watched it. It does not get old.
They walk in a party and leave a brawl. Or do not leave at all, small one. Delicious work — and the dungeon did it without spending a single minion.
Ah. A Hall of Madness. Heroes walk in trusting one another and walk out — if they do — having tried to kill one another, small one.

throne_room.t: Mm. A Throne Room — it spawns a Mini-Boss that scales with you, small one. A scaling guard is a guard for the long run.
The Throne Room placed. A garrison champion, room-bound but mighty, small one. Give the champion its solitude — and its kill-path.
A lesser throne for a greater guard, small one. Put it on the road to YOU — let the heroes meet a smaller you before the real one.

sanctum.t: Ah. A Sanctum — the capstone, small one. Your boss regenerates between fights, and the connected minions too. A long way to reach this room.
The Sanctum placed. Healing for the throne and its neighbours, small one. A dungeon that mends itself is a dungeon that endures.
Your final unlock, small one. A room that mends what the heroes break. ...Well built. I give little praise. Take this freely given.

skeleton.t: Mm. A skeleton — cheap, brittle, endlessly replaceable, small one. The dungeon's bread and bone. Buy plenty. Quantity has its own quiet menace.
Another skeleton. They die easy and rise easier, small one. Beautifully expendable. I have a long memory; I will not waste it on this one. Place more.
A skeleton placed. Not much alone, small one — but the dead are never alone for long in here. Stack them. The dead stack so obligingly.

orc.t: Ah. An orc. Brutish, proud, and it sharpens itself on every kill, small one. Feed it heroes and it simply gets worse. Worse, here, is the goal.
An orc joins the pit. Pack them close, small one — orcs fight fiercer shoulder to shoulder. Even brutes understand numbers. You should too.
An orc placed — all muscle and grudge, small one. The heroes will not enjoy this one. Good. The dungeon was never built for their enjoyment.

goblin.t: Mm. A goblin — small, shrieking, best in numbers, small one. One is a nuisance. Ten is a genuine problem. Build toward ten.
A goblin in the halls. Weak, fast, gleefully spiteful, small one. Cheap chaos. I will take cheap chaos — it costs little and irritates much.
Another goblin. Do not expect heroics, small one — expect a swarm that will not stop shrieking. The shrieking, too, is a kind of weapon.

imp.t: Ah. An imp — a quick, vicious scrap of the inferno, small one. It enjoys the work. So do I, in my slower way. Loose it on something.
An imp placed. Fast and nasty, small one. The heroes always underestimate the little ones — and the large ones too, eventually. Let them.
An imp joins us. Hellish, restless, and — for once — on your side, small one. Use the speed. I have none to spare; borrow the imp's.

ghost.t: Mm. A ghost. It drifts through walls and unnerves the living, small one. Eerie. Effective. I approve of both, and of things that ignore doors.
A ghost placed. No door stops it, no hero relaxes near it, small one. Lovely. Dread that patrols itself costs you nothing to maintain.
Another ghost for the halls. The heroes hate fighting what they cannot corner, small one. Lean into that hate. Hate makes them careless.

slime.t: Ah. A slime. It does not look like much, small one — and that, precisely, is the point. Underestimation is a trap the heroes set themselves.
A slime placed. Squishy, patient, underestimated to death, small one. Patient things are my favourite things. This one and I have an understanding.
Another slime. Charmingly disgusting, small one. Park it where some fool must squelch through. The maze should be unpleasant in every sense.

elder_slime.t: Mm. An elder slime — a slime that ate well and grew opinions, small one. Sturdier than it looks. Respect the ooze. Age improves most things. ...Most.
An elder slime placed. Years of slow digestion made it large and grim, small one. The old ones always hold the line — we are stubborn that way.
Another elder slime. The patient become the dangerous, small one. I would know — I am a very large, very patient example of the same principle.

golem.t: Ah. A golem. Slow as a grudge and twice as hard to budge, small one. Park it where the heroes MUST pass. Immovability is largely my strategy.
A golem placed — a wall that strikes back, small one. The heroes will tire long before it does. Outlasting the enemy. I have built an existence on it.
Another golem. Tremendous, tireless, immovable, small one. The dungeon's stubborn heart. I like it. It reminds me, a little, of myself.

beholder.t: Mm. A beholder minion — all eyes and menace, small one. Shield it well or lose it. A fragile thing well-placed is still formidable.
A beholder placed. Floating spite, small one. Keep something solid between it and the heroes. Protect what does your killing — simple husbandry.
Another beholder. It need not be close to be cruel, small one. My favourite trait in anything — cruelty that keeps its distance.

lich.t: Ah. A lich minion. Undead, spiteful, clever — death taught it patience, small one. The dead make excellent students. They have nothing but time.
A lich placed. A caster that does not breathe and does not tire, small one. A dreadful little scholar. I keep books too. We will get along.
Another lich for the halls. The heroes fear the thinking dead most of all, small one. Use that fear. A frightened hero is already half-defeated.

lizardman.t: Mm. A lizardman. Venom on every strike, small one — it need not win the fight, only START the poison. Patience again. The maze runs on it.
A lizardman placed. Scaled, toxic, patient, small one. The heroes die slow, and far from here. A kill that finishes off-stage is still a kill.
Another lizardman. One bite, and the clock begins, small one. I do love a slow knife. The hero spends the whole walk home arguing with it.

gnoll.t: Ah. A gnoll — the pack instinct runs deep, small one. Cluster them, and the snarling arithmetic adds up quickly. Numbers. The oldest math there is.
A gnoll placed. Hungry, vicious, far better in numbers, small one. Give it brothers. Many. A lone gnoll is a nuisance; a pack is a verdict.
Another gnoll. The pack grows, small one. The pack always wants to grow. Let it. Hunger, properly fed, becomes an army.

demon.t: Mm. A demon minion — a scrap of the inferno on the payroll, small one. Cruel by nature, loyal by contract. I have shared the deep dark with their kind.
A demon placed. Hellfire and bad intentions, small one. My kind and demons get along well enough — we both keep our promises, and both regret nothing.
Another demon for the halls. It enjoys the work, small one — and so will you, watching it. Loose it where the work is bloodiest.

ent.t: Ah. An ent — a walking wall of old wood, small one. Tremendous patience, tremendous endurance. The forest remembers; the forest does not hurry.
An ent placed. The heroes will tire of hacking long before it falls, small one. Let them swing. Every wasted swing is a small gift to the dark.
Another ent. Slow, ancient, immovable, small one. The forest holds a grudge — and a grudge that old has real weight. Aim the grudge well.

plant.t: Mm. A vinekin — rooted, thorny, dreadfully underestimated, small one. Let it grow on someone unfortunate. The garden in this dungeon bites.
A vinekin placed. Quiet until it is suddenly not, small one. The heroes never watch the shrubbery. They should. They will not. Profit from that.
Another vinekin. Cheap, green, full of thorns, small one. The maze should be unpleasant to touch as well as to walk. This one handles the touching.

mushroom.t: Ah. A mushroom minion. Spores and spite — it poisons the very air around it, small one. Cruelty you cannot see is cruelty the heroes cannot dodge.
A mushroom placed. Keep the heroes breathing near it, small one. A lovely, lingering rot. The dungeon should attack the lungs as readily as the flesh.
Another mushroom. Fungal and patient, small one. The dungeon does love a slow poison — and so, after ten thousand patient years, do I.

rat.t: Mm. A rat. One rat is nothing, small one. A NEST of rats is an entirely different conversation. Build the nest. Build it large.
A rat placed. Cheap, quick, disposable, small one. Drown the heroes in the small things. Enough small things become one large, fatal thing.
Another rat. Vermin by the handful, small one. Glory never held a corridor — numbers did. Glory is for ballads; numbers are for keeping.

zombie.t: Ah. A zombie. Slow, witless, utterly relentless, small one. It does not tire and it does not stop. ...A little like me, in honesty. I respect it.
A zombie placed. No speed, no cleverness — just an endless, shuffling NO, small one. The briefly-lived cannot match relentlessness. This one can.
Another zombie. The heroes can outrun it, small one. What they cannot do is outlast it. The dungeon has all the time the heroes do not.

vampire_minion.t: Mm. A vampire thrall. It drinks what it strikes, small one. Every blow it lands feeds it — the heroes weaken, it does not. An economy of blood. Tidy.
A thrall placed. Charming, pale, parasitic, small one. Put it in a long fight and watch it thrive. The longer the fight, the more this one likes it.
Another vampire thrall. It turns a brawl into a meal, small one. Loyal AND self-sufficient. It asks for nothing and gives back blood.

shooting_arrows.t: Mm. Shooting Arrows — wall-mounted, line-of-sight, lingering poison, small one. Aim the lane well. A trap aimed at a wall is merely decor.
An arrow trap placed. It fires forever and never breaks, small one. The perfect servant — tireless, silent, and entirely without ambition.
Shooting Arrows. A small sting, a slow poison, small one. The cruelty is in the WAIT. I respect a patient trap. I am, after all, a patient creature.

bomb.t: Ah. A Bomb — a few breaths of fuse, then ruin for everything within five tiles, small one. Your minions included. The blast does not read loyalties.
A bomb placed. One use, enormous noise, small one. Put it where a crowd will gather. Then enjoy the gathering's sudden end.
A Bomb. It does not discriminate — heroes and your own dead alike, small one. Place it boldly, or do not place it. Half-courage wastes a bomb.

cannon.t: Mm. A Cannon — wrought iron that fires down its line, forever, small one. Aim it cruelly. Its whole virtue is the angle you give it.
A cannon placed. A cannonball for any hero in its sights, small one. I value a thing that never tires — I outlived all that did.
A Cannon. Heavy, blunt, endless, small one. Point it down a corridor the heroes cannot avoid, and let the iron do the talking.

spike_pillar.t: Ah. A Spike Pillar. It spits spikes at anything within a tile, small one. No subtlety — and I do not always want subtlety. I want results.
A spike pillar placed. A blunt little horror, small one — it bites everything that crowds it. Put it where the heroes must crowd.
A Spike Pillar. All puncture, no manners, small one. The heroes will give it a frightened berth. Make every berth they take a longer, costlier walk.

dragon_trap.t: Mm. A Dragon Trap — wall-mounted, breathes fire down its line. Quaint, small one. The trap does not tire as I now do. Use it.
A dragon trap placed. The maze just learned to spit flame, small one. Aim it where they cannot dodge.
A Dragon Trap. The nastiest wall in the dungeon, small one. It is named for my kind — I take that as the compliment it is.

spike_pit.t: Ah. A Spike Pit — hidden until the heroes step onto it, small one. Heavy damage, and a chance to simply END a hero. The maze's quiet executioner.
A spike pit placed. Patient, invisible, sometimes lethal in a single instant, small one. Tuck it deep. The deep traps catch the confident ones.
A Spike Pit. They will not see it until they are IN it, small one. I have a great respect for a thing that kills without a sound.

rotating_blades.t: Mm. Rotating Blades — forever spinning, forever hungry for anything in reach, small one. It does not aim. Relentlessness rarely needs to.
A blade trap placed. It does not stop and it does not think, small one — it simply cuts. Lovely. A pure, uncomplicated cruelty.
Rotating Blades. A wall of whirling teeth, small one. The heroes must thread it, or bleed for it. Most of them, in my long experience, bleed.

saw_blade.t: Ah. A Saw Blade — it travels its track endlessly, slicing any hero it overlaps, small one. Make them dance. I do enjoy watching the heroes dance.
A saw blade placed. A walkable track, an unwalkable timing, small one. Cruelty with a metronome. The maze keeps such excellent rhythm.
A Saw Blade. Heavy damage, and it shares the floor and ruins it anyway, small one. Place it mean. A trap placed politely is a trap placed wrong.

door_lock.t: Mm. A Door Lock — it seals the way until a hero finds the key, small one. Anything that costs heroes TIME costs them the day.
A door lock placed. It pairs with a Key Chest somewhere, small one — guard that chest, or this lock is mere theatre. Make the delay long.
A Door Lock. Funnel them, frustrate them, make them HUNT for the way through, small one. A hunting hero is a slow hero, and slow is how the maze wins.

treasure_chest.t: Ah. A Treasure Chest. Passive gold each day — and a shining temptation the greedy cannot refuse, small one. Bait that pays you to be bait.
A chest placed. It pays you nightly and lures raid groups, small one. Guard it well — or do not, and feast on what the gold attracts.
A Treasure Chest, small one. Bait that turns a profit. The best lies are the profitable ones — I have built a hoard on exactly that principle.

soul_bound_beacon.t: Mm. A Soul-Bound Beacon — and its Healing Fountain, small one. A safe haven the heroes will trust. ...Foolishly. Surround the mercy with teeth.
A beacon placed, paired with a fountain, small one. A tempting little oasis. Make the oasis a trap. Nothing here should be as kind as it looks.
A Soul-Bound Beacon. It LOOKS like mercy, small one. The dungeon does not do mercy — and neither, after ten thousand years, do I. It does bait.

phylactery_heart.t: Ah. The Phylactery Heart — your hidden spare life, small one. Place it where no hero would think to look. Carelessly hidden is no spare at all.
The Heart is placed, small one. A fourth life, beating in the dark. I have kept secrets for ages — the trick is to never, ever point at them.
Your Phylactery Heart, small one. While it beats, death is a mere inconvenience. Lose it carelessly, and... mm. Do not. I dislike repeating myself.
